Guiana, 1890; ag. survr., 1896; 5th cls. elk., 1897; 3rd grade survr., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., 1904; ag. 1st cls. offr., 1901 and 1906; J.P. for the col., 1906; ag. warden, No. 3 mining dist., 1906, 1907 and 1909; 1st cls. offr., 21st Sept., 1908; ag. forestry offr., 1914-15; performed duties of prin. elk. in head off., 1916; represented Col. at Imp. Forestry Conf., Canada, 1923; seconded for duty with B. Guiana exhibit, Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924; supt., forest surveys, forestry dept., 1926.

HOLDBROOK, HUBERT GEORGE.—B. 1887; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1908; B.B.Ch., B.A.C., 1910; M.D., 1923; L.M., Retunda, med. offr., S. Stltmts., 1912; med. offr., F.M.S., 1913; certif., L.S.T.M., 1921; health offr., F.M.S., 1921; ch. med., offr., Malacca, 1923; ag. senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, 1924; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, 1926; senr. med. offr., Selangor, Feb., 1927.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stltmts., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engrnr., Malacca, Dec., 1909; ag. exec. engrnr., Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1910; ag. exec. engrnr., Singapore, June, 1913; ag. exec. engrnr., Malacca, Mar., 1915; ag. exec. engrnr., Singapore, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engrnr., Mar., 1920; exec. engrnr., Malacca, 29th Dec., 1920.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1889; on milly. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. olk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct. 1921.

HOLE, COMMDR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Petrel," 1914-16; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbrmr., Hong Kong, 1924; commdr., 1927.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1911; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Mishahoe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee regis. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-28.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, B.A. (Lond.), F.R. Hist. S.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. Johns' Schi., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmtr., teacher's training schi., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1923; author "Milangoya History" (Vols. I and II); "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schi. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab), 2nd cls. hist., tripes; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1910; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., Jan., 1913; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., C. Prov., Nov., 1922.

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., A.P.E., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admtn. and priv. sec. to H.M. comsnr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admtn., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1906-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to gov't., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. gov't., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional adminstr., German E. Africa (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov', Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Trinidad, 1929; author of works on the Masai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-29; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928.

HOLM, ALEXANDER, C.B.E. (1926), M.R.A.S.R. (Gold Medallist), F.H.A.S., S.E.A.C. (Dip. Hosp.).—Gen. manager, govt. experimental farm, Potchefstroom, Transvaal, 1903; gen. manager and principal, schl. of agric., Potchefstroom, 1909; under sec. for agric. (educltn.), Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 1918; mem., exec. leg. and inter-col. rly. couns.; pres., agric. and horticl. society of Kenya; Br. Empire Exhibn. comsnr. for Kenya, 1924.

HOLMES, HAROLD KENNARD, C.B.E. (1928).—Ed. All Saints, Bloxham; admitted solr., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1900; served with K.R.R.C., Somme, 1916; wounded, Guillelmont; with 20th London Regt. at Salonika, Palestine, France and Rhine, 1917-18, 2nd lieut., 1918; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1919; J.P., 1919; ag. land offr., 1921 to 1922; ag. crown solr., 1922; crown solr., 1922.

HOLMES, ROBERT BLAKE WORSLEY, O.B.E., M. Inst. C.E., M.I.M.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1878; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schi. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. engrnr., Lagos rly. (constrn.), Nigeria, 1900; asst. loco. supt., Nigeria rly., wks. man., dist. loco. supt., dep.ch. mech. engrnr., Nigerian Eastern Rly., 1905-16; formed mech. and shipyard formations, inland waterways and docks br., R.E., Richborough, 1916; major, Sept., 1914; lieut.-col., Mar., 1917; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E., 1918; col., Mar., 1918; demob., Apr., 1919; recalled to colours, June, 1919, and proceeded to Damascus as asst. dir., rly. traffic, in ch. of Hejaz rly., Nov., 1919; in ch., evacuation of troops by rail from Syria, Dec., 1919; col., grade T., Mar., 1920; gen. man., Palestine mily. rly., Mar., 1920; transf'd. to civ. govt. as gen. man., Palestine rly., Oct., 1920; col., R. of O., Nov., 1921.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Arty., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., O.O., 7th Feb., 1921; B.A. (lit. hum.) July, 1921; pvte. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926; asst. prte. sec. to S. of S. for dom. affrs. and for cols. (Mr. L. S. Amery), Feb., 1928; accomp. Sir S. Wilson on visit to W. African cols., Aug. to Nov., 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 24th Jan., 1930.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; asst. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; POL. MAG., Gampola, Dec., 1926; ast. pvt. sec. to gov., Mar., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1927; office ast., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing surv., cust., Mar., 1928.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney). Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schol. of Mines (London) in Mining, Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schol. of Mines, 1901; mining engr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HONE, CAPT. RALPH HERBERT, M.C., LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. Vardean Schol., Brighton and London Univ.; barrister-at-law; enlisted, Inns of Court O.T.C., 1915; 2nd lieut., 18th Lond. Regt., July, 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Jan., 1918; attd. H.Q. 60th Divn., Sept., 1918; adjt., 1/18 Lond. Regt., Feb., 1916; served twice B.E.F., France 1916 and 1917-18; capt. R. of O. (T.A.), 1922; ast. treas., Uganda, Sept., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple; LL.B. hon., Class II (Lond.), July, 1926; offl. recr. in bankcy. and regar., H.B.M.s high ct., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. res. mag., Zanzibar, Jan., 1926 to Feb., 1927; res. mag., Zanzibar, Mar., 1928; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929.

HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedit. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with H.C. Africa admtn., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acctng. offr., Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admtn., Mar., 1907; res. comnr., Swaziland, Jan., 1917; gov., Seychelles, 1928.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Schol. and R. Vety Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vetry. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLESS, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hon., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associationship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; ast. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. sup't, schls., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; ast. dir., educn., 1927.

HOOGENHOUT, NICOLAAS MARAIS.—B. 1876; ed. Boys' High Schol., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; M.A. (mod. lang.), Univ. of Cape of Good Hope; Ph.D., Kaiser-Wilhelms-Universitat, Strasbourg (Alsace); prin., Eendracht High Schol. Pretoria, 1904; vice-prin., Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1908; prin., ditto, 1921; rector, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1925; dir., educn., Transvaal, 1928.

HOOGENHOUT, P. IMKER.—B. 1884; ed. Stellenbosch and Cape Town, B.A. and prof. teachers' certifs. 1904 and 1906; headmtr., Jeppie Educnl. Inst., Johannesburg, 1908, and Hoogenhout High Schol., Bethal, 1910; inspr., educn., S.-W. Transvaal, 1921; ch. examr. to Trans. educn. dept., 1927; sec. for interior, 1929.

HOOKER, RICHARD SYMONDS.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Gram. Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; passed lower standard exam. in Mende; ast. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, July, 1910; dist. comnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comnr., N. Prov., Nov., 1920 to Aug., 1921 and from Sept., 1922; prov. comnr., Jan., 1926.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCHLOT.—B. 1876; ed. at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Schol. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in desps., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vol.; ag. col. surg. res., Penang, June, 1905; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah to Nov., 1920; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigr., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kedah, in addition, Nov., 1917; prin. civil med. offr., S. Stltlns., 23rd Nov., 1920; pres. coun., coll. of med., S'pore.; pres., S.S. and F.M.S. med. council; mem., leg. coun., S. Stltlns., 1st Jan., 1923; ehmn., tuberculosis comte., June to Aug., 1923; pres., 6th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Singapore, Sept., 1923.

HOPE, JAMES.—B. 1839; ed. Potchefstroom Coll., Transvaal and R.M.C., Sandhurst; gaz. 2nd lieut., K.O.S.B., 1918; Br.-Gen. Serv. and Victory Medals; joined Swaziland serv., Sept., 1919; gaz. olk. to A.C. Hlatikulu; ag. mast., regist. and sheriff, 1923; con. olk. to treas., N. Rhodesia, June, 1929; olk., Bech. Prot., May, 1927; passed 3rd grade Swazi language exam., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv., lower law exam., 1922; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1920.
HOPKINS, J. G.—Ast. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILIUS, C.M.G. (1922), D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical ast., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in desps., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling comnr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; travelling comnr., North Bank Prov., Gambia, 1921; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. ast., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treas., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto, 1923; 3rd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926; sec., comnr., of currency, 1928.
HOPPS, HERBERT DAVIDSON.—B. 1876; ast. foreman, Indian State Rly., 1903; foreman, 1919; E. African Forces, 1914-19; ast. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rly., Oct., 1919.

HOPTON, REGINALD EDWARD, L.R.C.I. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.).—B. 1887; surg. probationer, R.N.V.R. (on active serv.), 1916-19; house surx., R. Infirmary, Edin., 1921; clin. ast., Grovenor Hosp. for woman, London, 1923; clin.-ast., London Lock Hosp., 1925; specialist in venereal diseases, Cyprus, July, 1927; ag. dir., health, Sept., 1929.

HORB-RUTHVEN, MAJOR-GEN. HON. SIR ALEXANDER GORE ARKWRIGHT, K.C.M.G. (1928), V.C. (1899), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Eton; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Infy., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1898; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent operations. (V.C., 4th cls. Osmanlieh, English and Egyptian medics, with clasp, three times mentioned in despatches.) sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroy of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov.-gen., C'wealth of Australia, 1908; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1914-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., mentioned in despatches five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brig. (Guards); 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1923), M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904 and has served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; rec'd thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr., ad mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comm. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and sanitary services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sanitary Comttee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; consulting physician to C.O., 1928; mem., exec. comttee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY ERTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schi. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agr. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in despatches; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schi. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922.

HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER, O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schi.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1909; offr.-in-charge, Masai res., May, 1923; senr. commr. (2nd class), Kenya, Jan., 1924; senr. commr., 1st grade, 1926.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schi.; C.A., Jan., 1908; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engng. contracts, 1920; tech. offr., "Discovery Comttee," 1923.
HORSFIELD, WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Denstone and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hon. mech. sci. tripos, 1921; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921.
HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., despatches; dist. polit. eff., Somaliland, 1919; dist. commr., Somaliland, 1922.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schi., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 13th Aug., 1919; instr. of metals, Accra tech. schi., 10th Apr., 1920; ag. prin., 1st Dec., 1920 to 22nd Mar., 1921; house mast., 6th Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schi., 28th Apr., 1926.

HORWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. B.A. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Field Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1896; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1896, on the subject of confederation with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun. and of treaty board; acted as leader of govt. during seas. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comm. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newf. centre of St. John Ambulance Assoc'tn.; chrmn. of comttee, for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newf. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comm. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSEGOOD, THOMAS WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1886; younger brother of Trinity House master mariner; ed. St. Paul's Cathedral Choir Schi.; habrm'r. and naval agt., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. ch. of pol. on various occasions; ass't habrm'r., marine mag., etc., Hong Kong, 1927.
HOSKING, K.B.—B. 1890; ed. King's Schi. Canterbury and Wadham Coll., Oxford (clae schi.); asst. dist. commr., E.A.F.P., Dec., 1915; dist. commr., Kenya, 1925.

HOUGHTON, H. E.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high commr. of S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transferred to Basutoland serv., Nov. 1923.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv., lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical asst., Basutoland, 5th Oct., 1910; sub-inpnr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regis., 1st July, 1917; served as 2nd lieu. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; insp. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1919; dep. asst. commr., 1923.

HOWARD, ADDERLEY FITZALAN BERNARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Coll.; on active serv., Durham L.I., 1914-19; ret. with hon. rank of capt., Mar., 1919; M.C., "1914-15," star and two war medals, ment. in despatches, 1917 and 1918; col. audit dept., May, 1919; asst. audnr., Nigeria, July, 1919; audnr., Gambia, Oct., 1926; 2nd asst. audnr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928.

HOWARD, HON. ERATUS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Schi., Inverness Acad., etc.; McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in maths. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist), 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1918; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 2nd... Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof., civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. astt. acctnt., army accts. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. astt. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. astt. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supernny. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation commn. treas., as astt. contr. of depl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921; prin., 27th Aug., 1928.

HOWARD, JOHN CURROIS.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhib.) honours, nat. sci.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1918; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; pres. dist. st., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1921; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1925; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1927-29.

HOWARD-FIANDERS, WILLIAM EDGAR.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1927; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-18; called to bar, M.T., 1923; mem., south-eastern circ. and cent. crim. ct.; mag., St. Vincent, June, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1928; income tax comnr., 1928; mem., educ. bd., 1928; ag. col. sec., July, 1928; atty.-gen., Sept., 1928.

HOWARTH, SAMUEL EDWARD JAMES.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War; Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; astt. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; astt. acctnt., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1906; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1916.
HOWATSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1861; dist. med. offr., Trinidad, 15th May, 1905.

HOWELL, ALLRYNE GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk. corrpdg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. comnr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1906, and 1907.

HOWELL, CHARLES GOUGH.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., Cantab.; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-17; served in France and Flanders, May to Dec., 1915 and ret. on account of wounds in Aug., 1917; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1921; mem., S. Wales circuit; crown coun., Kenya, Nov., 1925.

HOWELL, E. H., M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; depl. astt., 16th Jan., 1923; clk. for legal instruments, 26th Feb., 1923.

HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th cls., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. coun. and exec. comtee., 1925.

HOWELL, JOHN BRUCE, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treasy., Barbados, July, 1880; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comtee., Jan., 1898; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados in 1902, 1904, 1906, 1907, 1910 and 1911; J.P., 1918; ch. clk., col. secy.'s off., Aug., 1918; ag. col. sec. on various occasions 1922-25; has held various other ag. apptmts.; man., govt. savings bank, 1925.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offi. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1927 and Sept. to Oct., 1928.

HUBBALI, JOHN GORST.—Clk., control and audit office, Cape, 1896; examnr., 1895; astt. acctnt., 1903; astt. acctnt., contrlr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treasy., Union of S.Africa, 1916; sec., office of high comnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1928.
HUDDEN, EDWARD LANPHIER.—B. 1902; ed. Radley Coll.; lieut., 4th Battn. Gloucestershire Regiment, T.A., 1925; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1925.

HUDSON, HAROLD.—Book-keeper, treasy. dept., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1911; cashier, treasy. dept., Aug., 1911; astt. treas., Mar., 1914; 1st grade astt. treas., Jan., 1922; ag. deputy treas., June to Dec., 1919, Dec., 1921 to Feb., 1922, Sept., 1922 to Apr., 1923, May, 1923 to Mar., 1924, Feb. to Oct., 1925, Mar. to Dec., 1926, and from Mar. to July, 1927.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mil'y. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; addt. to Colombo kach., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitoya, Mar., 1921; astt. settlmt. offr., 1922; office astt., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; astt. settlmt. offr., May, 1925.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. privatel.; and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff, cent. telegraphs, London, 1902; dept., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tele., occupied enemy territory admstrn., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUGGARD, WALTER CLARENCE, K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honours man and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. puisne judge, June-Oct., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; sovr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. puisne judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.O., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1923; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revd. edn. Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1925; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec. and gov't. dep., Aug., 1927; atty.-gen., S. Sttlnts., June, 1929.

HUGGINS, CAPT. JOHN, M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schol. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov. 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July 1st, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., 27th Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, 28th May, 1920; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 7th July, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, 7th Oct., 1920; passed exam. in law, Mar., 1921; 4th ast. sec. to govt., 9th Mar., 1921; passed final exam., Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Kajang, 24th Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1926; ag. 2nd ast. sec. (A), S.S., Feb., 1926; pvt. sec. to high commr., May, 1926; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. III., Aug., 1928.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1906; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Kelantan, Dec., 1910; mag., Kota Bharu, Sept., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1914; ag. ast. adviser, Kelantan, Jan., 1919; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Oct., 1922; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, June, 1923; offr., cls. I., Dec., 1923; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1927; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1927; ag. commr., lands, S.S., Aug., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1928; offr., cls. IB, commr., lands, F.M.S., continues ag. as above, July, 1928.

HUGHES, LESLIE DONALD CHARLETON, M.C.—B. 1890; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1914; attd. ch., censor's office, Apr., 1915; attd. Badulla kach., Oct., 1915; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, March, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917-19; ast. dir., food prodn., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1921; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1923; ast. attlit. offr., Nov., 1923; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1928.

HUGHES, BRIG.-GEN. ROBERT HERBERT WILFRED, C.B. (1921), C.S.I. (1918), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1916), Officer Legion of Honour (1917)—B. 1872; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1886-87; merchant service, 1887-1898; Bd. of T. master mariner's certif.; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1896; lieut., 1904; comdr., 1914; capt., 1919; served in S. African war, in S.A. Light Horse, Bethune's M.I., and Royal Navy, from 12th Dec., 1899 to 1st May, 1902; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 27th March, 1903; qualified in surveying at R.G.S. in 1906; elected a younger Brother of Trinity House, 1909; senr. marine offr., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. marine supt. and harbr.-mast., Lagos, 1st July to 10th Dec., 1906; commanded Nigerian govt. yacht "Ivy" as armed auxiliary under the R.N. in the Cameroons Expdn., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1916; Trinity House pilot in N. Sea, Apr.-June, 1916; seconded to Army as Major, R.K., 19th June, 1916; lieut.-col., Sept., 1916; brig.-gen., May, 1917; dir. Inland Water Transport, Mespot. till Apr. 1921; demob., 13th Apr. 1921; dep. dir. of marine, Nigeria, Aug., 1921; dir. of marine, Oct., 1921.

HUGHES, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs, 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., 27th Oct., 1915; ditto, 14th Nov., 1916; ditto, 17th Feb., 1917; ditto, 10th Jan., 1918; resigned, Feb., 1923.
HUMPHERSON, LESLIE HERBERT.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Schol., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 7th Jan., 1925.

HUMPHREYS, LIEUT.-COL. SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.V.O. (1928), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. African War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); cent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; prte sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., 1904; ast. commr., Hazara, 1907; dep. commr., Bannu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N. Waziristan, 1915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (despa.); served European War in Europe as pilot R.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khyber, 1919; dep. foreign sec. to govt. of India, 1921; 1st envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary at the court of H.M. the King of Afghanistan, Jan., 1922; high commr., Iraq, 1929.

HUMPHREYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl. Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treasy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treasy., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acctn., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to Jul., 1912; ag. treasur. of Antigua, and federal treas. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, BERTIE LEONARD.—B. 1883; ed. privately and Queen's Coll., London; trooper, 5th batt. Australian C'wealth Horse, S. African War, 1901-02; Ca e Mounted Rifles, 1902-03; B.S.A. Co.'s mounted pol., 1904; various clkships, N. Rhodesia, 1907-10; acctn. P.W.D., July, 1910; acctn., London offices, B.S.A. Co., May, 1913; seconded to B.S.A. Co.'s comm'l branch, N. Rhodesia, 1914-21; acctn., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1921; sec., dept. of lands, Apr., 1924.
HUNT, HENRY AMBROSE.—B. 1866; F.R. Met. Soc.; meteorologist, C. of A., 1906.

HUNTER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR ARCHIBALD, K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1886).—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Royal Lancaster, 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1889; col., 1894; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Egypt, and twice wounded; gov. of Dongola Prov. and commdt. Frontier Field Force, 1895-99; gov. of Omdurman, 1899; Egyptian medal and her orders; in command of 1st cls. dist. in India; lieut.-gen. on staff, commanded 10th div. S.Africa, 1900-01 (twice ment. in despa.); commanded in Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corps, India, 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, India Army, 1907-9; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1910-1913.
HUNTER, SIR GEORGE, Kt. BACH. (1921).—B. 1860; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1896-99, and since 1911.

HUNTER, JAMES BLAKE, B.A. (Univ. of Toronto).—B. 1876; clk., priv. coun. off., Canada, 1899; transf. to P.O. dept., 1900; marine and fisheries, 1901; pub. wks., 1902 ch. clk., 1907; deputy min. of pub. wks., 1908.

HUNTER, JAMES STANLEY.—B. 1889; ed. Hawera High Sch.; entd. N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; post and tel. dept., 1905; pub. serv. commr.'s office, 1914; pvt. sec. to various ministers, 1916-24; offr., sec., N.Z. rly., 1924; asst. gen. man., N.Z. rly., 1928; commr., transport, 1929; pvt. sec. to Sir Francis Bell at League of Nations gen. assem. and also at Imp. Conf., 1928.
HUNTER, JOHN.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; clk. to patronage comte., Nov., 1907.

HUNTER, JOHN ADAMS.—B. 1890; ed. at Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (hist. tripods cls. 2, I.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; ag. supervisor, cust., Port Dickson, Dec., 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Aug.-Dec., 1917; in India studying Tamil, 1918; offr., els. V, 26th Sept., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 6th Oct., 1918; attd. to chief sec.'s office, Jan.-Mar., 1919; seconded for serv. in food control dept., 26th Mar., 1919; ag. 3rd asst. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., 7th Oct., 1920; 4th asst. sec. to Govt., 3rd Oct., 1921; offr., els. IV., offp. dep. contr., lab., Seremban, Nov., 1921; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; on spl. duty, rubber census, July, 1923; offr. in ch. gov't. toddy shops in addn., July, 1923; 3rd asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Oct., 1926; offr., Class III, Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1927.
HUNTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

HUNTER, LOUIS LUCIEN.—B. 1889; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., 1914; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; addl. commr., requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1915; addl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Pana- ture, Sept., 1916; office ast. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chiaw, sept., 1919; addl. ast. gov't. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1922; ast. sttlm. offr., Jan., 1923; addl. ast. dir., edcn., July, 1928.
HURST, RAYMOND GEORGE.—B. 1877; ast. state rly., 1900; E. African rly., 1917; ast. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., July, 1925.

HUSSEY, ERIC ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1885; ed., tepton (Sohl.), open exhib., Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1904; B.A., 1908 (lit. hum.), M.A., 1923; Sudan civ. serv. as tutor, Gordon Coll., Khartum, sept., 1908; dist. commr., 1912; ch. inspr., ducn. dept., 1918; edcnl. mission to Somaliland, 1920; edcnl. mission to Uganda, 1924; dir., ducn., Uganda, 1925; edcnl. mission to Kenya, 1928; dir. edcn., Nigeria, 1929.

HUTCHINGS, HUGH H.—Inspr. of gov't. chls., Turks and Caicos Is., 1898-1906; J.P., 1901; h. offr., fire brigade, 1901-19; clk. to commr. and inspr. of schls., 1907; ast. commr., Grand Turf, 1908-19; ag. audr., 1908-13; audr., 1913-19; g. commr. and ag. judge of sup. ct., five occasions, 1911-19; ag. commr. of Cayman Is., 1912-13; commr., Cayman Is., June, 1919; commr., Montserrat, Sept., 1929.

HUTCHINS, ERNEST ELWIN.—B. 1886; ed., Exeter Sch.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 1903-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest de- marcator, Kenya, 1909; ast. conservator, forests, Kenya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-16; ast. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; admstve.-offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MENTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Mily. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Min. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924.

HUTCHISON, DONALD MAXWELL WRIGHT, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; ed. Wellington Coll. and Faraday House, London; ast. elec. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1911; ch. inspr., elec. enact., F.M.S., 1915; dep. ch. engnr., electl. bd., P.W.D., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. elec. engnr. and inspr., electl. bd., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1926; ch. elec. engnr. and inspr., electl. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ag. dir., elec. dept., June to Dec., 1928.

HUTSON, SIR ETHE, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), B.A.—B. 1864; 2nd offr. to gov't. Leeward Is., Oct., 1886; ast. priv. sec. to gov't. Barbados, Nov., 1885; priv. sec. to gov't. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and regisr.-gen., Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comm., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour commn., 1903; col. sec. Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., educn. commn., Apr., 1909; represented Fiji and Western Pacific high comm., at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep. gov't., May and Oct., 1909, Sept., 1910, Mar., Sept., Oct., 1911, and June, 1912; commr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov't. of Fiji and high commr. for Western Pacific, Aug., 1915 to May, 1916 and from June to Oct., 1918; gov't., B. Honduras, Nov., 1918; gov', Fiji and high commr., W. Pacifica, 1925; ret., July, 1929.

HUTTON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS.—B. 1862; ed. Bedford Public Sch., S. African Coll. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., 1883; LL.B., 1892; called to the bar, supreme ct., Cape Colony, 1892; prosecutor, special treason ct., 1901-3; judge, supreme ct., assigned to E.D. local divn., 1913; mem., special treason ct., 1915-16; mem. of rebellion enquiry commn., 1916-17; judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Feb., 1923.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, R.F.Hist.S.—B. 1875; ed. Oxford High Sch. and Swansea Gram. Sch.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1896-98; supplementary estab., secy.'s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of sec., miny. of infrn., Apr., 1918 to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; ast. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrl. of lab., Dec., 1921; in charge of dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrl. permits, Apr., 1924; ch. immigrn. offr., Oct., 1926; sec., cent. town planning commn., Sept., 1923; mem., economy comm., chmn., commte. on rates of wages for gov't. employees; dep. chmn., lab. standing commte.; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People," "Palestine, Old and New,” “British Projects for the Restoration of the Jews,” etc.

IEVERS, C.L., L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.T.M. (L'pool.). Applt. to W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Feb., 1912; Togoland, 5th to 26th Aug., 1914; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1918; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1921.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. regis., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

IM THURN, SIR EVERARD F., K.C.M.G. (1906), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxon; special mag., Pomeroon Dist., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1890; gov't. agt., N.W. dista., 1890; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Dec., 1899; col. sec., Ceylon, 1901; admstrd. gov't., Ceylon, 1902; gov't., Fiji, and high comnrs., W. Pacifico, June, 1904; ret. 1910; rep. Fiji, and W. Pacific at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; ed. of the Guiana scientific jnl., "Timshri," 1882-7; author of "Among the Indians of Guiana," 1883, and papers on the anthropology, natural history, geography, and history of Guiana; in 1884 made first ascent of Mount Roraima in the interior of Br. Guiana.

INGHAM, RAWWOOD, B.A. (Oxon).—Entered S. Sttlmts. civ. serv., cls. V. (passed in Chinese), Dec., 1912; addl. to monopolies dept., Jan., 1913; dep. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Sept., 1915; pol. mag., Malacca (in addn.), Nov., 1915; collr. war tax, Malacca (in addn.), Mar., 1917; collr., war tax, Singapore (tempy.), Mar., 1918; ag. supr. prisons, Malacca (in addn.), Mar.-June, 1919; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1919; ast. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1921.

INGLEDOW, CEDRIC FRANCIS.—B. 1894; on mly. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Aug., 1920; addl. to Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1920; ag. office astt. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office astt. to gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1924; ditto to gov't. agt., N. Prov., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, N'Eliya, Dec., 1927.

INGLES, F. H.—B. 1878; ed. at U.S. Coll., Westward Ho!, Newton Coll., Devon, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A. Cantab., 1902; ast. dist. comnrs., S. Nigeria, 6th Oct., 1906; dist. comnrs., 2nd grade, 17th Feb., 1913; dist. offr., 1st grade, 20th Oct., 1919; res., 5th Feb., 1922; ag. senr. res., 31st Oct., 1924.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., Sept., 1914 to May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., Dec., 1916; min. of national service, Dec., 1917; min. of lab., demob. and resettlement dept., Nov., 1918; eded. F.Z.S., May, 1919; ast. dist. comnrs., Zanzibar, July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Société d'Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif. acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; ag. dist. comnrs., Aug., to Sept., 1923; ag. regis., high ct., offl. assignee and regis., patents, designs and trade marks, Oct., 1923 to Feb., 1924; 2nd ast. sec., Jan., 1925; prte. sec. to ag. high comnrs., Apr. to June, 1925; ditto to Br. res., Nov., 1925 to Aug., 1926 and from Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. prte. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, Nov., 1925 to June, 1926; clk., prot. coun., Feb. to June, 1926; edr., Offl. Gaz., in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. senr. ast. sec., Mar. to Aug., 1926 and from Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; ag. ast. ch. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug. to Oct., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; mem. Pemba eduon. comittee and teaching of Koran coman., 1924; mem. museum comittee, 1924 and chmn., Aug., 1926; cls. IV., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar 1927; ast. ool. sec., Mauritius, 1927; chmn., museum reorganisation comittee, 1928; chmn., coman. of enquiry, med. and health dept., 1928; part author, "Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History" (1924), "Schl. History of Zanzibar" (1925), and "Aya Za Kurumaani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate" (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926) and various pamphlets and articles.
INMAN, HOWARD.—B. 1887; B. of T. Certif.: inspr. of weights and measures, G. Comst., 12th June, 1912.

INNES, CECIL BENNETT ROSE.—Clk., chief acctnt., rly., 1890; census, 1891; Brit. Bechuanaland serv.; Gordonia, Apr., 1891; relieving clk., Vryburg, Mar., 1892; clk. to P.M.G., Brit. Bechuanaland, May, 1892; sec. office, Apr., 1893; clk., regis. of deeds, Brit. Bechuanaland, Mar., 1894; deeds office, Vryburg, Sept., 1897; ag. regis., 20th July, 1900; clk. in charge, 1st Dec., 1901; ag. ast. magt., Laingburg, June, 1903; sub-coman., war losses compensation coman., June, 1903; clk. in charge, deeds office, Kimberley, Nov., 1903; rev. cl. civil coman., Cape, May, 1907; ch. clk., Sep., 1908; receiver of revenue, East London, Dec., 1910; principle clk., recvr. of revenue, Cape Town, Nov., 1916; ch. clk., recvr. of rec., Dec., 1923.

INNES, RT. HON., SIR JAMES ROSE, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ot., Cape Colony, 12th Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assm., Cape, 1884; subsequently reptrd. Cape div.; atty.-gen. 1st Rhodes' ministry, 17th July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comittee prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, 29th Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. just. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., 1st Mar., 1927.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, C.M.G. (1929), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Sohl. and St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; house physician, Bethlem Royal Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1905; senr. sanry. offr., Nigeria, 1914; ast. dir. of med. serv., Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and sanry. services, Sierra Leone, 1924; dir., med. and sanry. serv., Gold Coast, Mar., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns.
INNISS, KNOLLYS ULBRIC ALEXANDER, M.B. Ch. B. Edin.—B. 1882; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 16th Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1912.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY, O.B.E. (Mily.), Clk., adminstr.'s off., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to adminstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; dir. B.S.A. Co., Apr., 1919; gen. man., B.S.A. Co., S. Africa, June, 1922; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion 1896 (medal) and Rhodesia field force, 1900 (medal and 2 clasps); joined R.A.S.C., 1914; O.C., R.A.S.C., Harwich dist., 1914-17; S.S.O.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and Bucks. L.I., May, 1915; invalided with rank of capt., Jan., 1918; asst. priv. sec. (apptmnts.) to Lord Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, Feb., 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1903; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.Sc. Ag., Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918, R.S.C., agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, HON. SIR WM. HILL, K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.—B. 1885; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. H. of R., C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept., 1914; chief just., Victoria, 5th Apr., 1918; lieut.-gov., Victoria, 11th May, 1918.

IRVING, FRANCIS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Schl., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; served in S. African campaign; ch. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902; clk. to adminstr., O.F.S. Prov., 10th Aug., 1910; ch. clk. to prov. sec. (2nd grade), 16th Oct., 1924.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD, M.B.E. (1918).—Ed. at Charterhouse and Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S. African campaign; ch. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902; clk. to adminstr., O.F.S. Prov., 10th Aug., 1910; ch. clk. to prov. sec. (2nd grade), 16th Oct., 1924.
IRVING, HUGH CRAUFURD.—Asst. dist. comsrr., Somaliland Prot., June, 1914.

IRWIN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), HON. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD.—B. 1881; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls, Oxford (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragoons; M.P., Ripon divn., Yorks, 1910-25; under sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educn., Oct., 1922; viceroy of India, 1926.

IRWIN, HENRY MARK, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Haileybury and Selwyn Coll.; Cambridge exhibitioner; Haileybury exhibitioner; hons. class. ripos., 1908; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; attached to secretariat, July to Dec., 1911; priv. sec. to ag. gov., C. L. Temple, Esq., C.M.G., June, 1912.
IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1929.

ISAACS, RT. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1905-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927.

ISAACS, FRANK NOEL.—B. 1871; Jamaica civ. serv., 1890; held appts. in various depts., 1891-1907; olk. of wks. and acctnt., new pub. bldgs., 1899; sec., bd. of supervision, 1910; sec., malaria comsn., 1910-12; exec. offr., malaria comsn., 1910-12; drought and hurricane relief offr., 1912-17; ag. prot., immigrts., 1914; man., Rio Cobre home, 1917; comsrr., parish of St. Catharine, 1921; sec., child welfare comsn., 1921.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; loaned to polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika admtns., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. snr. comsrr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. coun., Mar. to Nov., 1927 and Aug., 1929; Swahili examr.

ISLINGTON, 1ST BARON (cr. 1910), Rt. Hon. SIR JOHN POYNDER DICKSON-POYNDER, 6TH BART., P.C. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1913); K.C.M.G. (1911); D.S.O. (1900); J.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; major and hon. col., Wilts Yeomanry; served in S. Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); late lieut., 3rd Royal Scots; mem. L.C.C., 1898-1904; M.P., Chippenham, Wilts, 1892-1910; gov., New Zealand, 1910-1912; chmn. of Indian civil ser. comsn., 1912; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 1914; parly. under sec. for India, 1915.
IVES, WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta., Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.
IZARD, GODFREY WALLACE.—B. 1891; ed. Uppingham; B.S.A. Co., 1904-14; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., 1914; invalided; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915.
IZARD, H.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.

JACKLIN, S., M.C.—Acctnt., repat. dept., Christiana, Aug., 1902; rev. clk., law dept., Nov., 1902; clk., civ. comsn. off., Pretoria, Nov., 1903; clk., rev. dept., Mar., 1906; prin. clk., Oct., 1906; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1912; inspr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. comsrr., Johannesburg, Sept., 1919; ch. clk., estabtns., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1st Jan., 1920; sec., pub. ser. comsrr., Oct., 1921; ret., 18th Oct., 1926; treas., League of Nations, 18th Oct., 1926.

JACKMAN, EDWARD CLARENCE.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. Middle Temple, 1889; lecture prizeman, real property, 1890; lecture prizeman, equity, 1891; called to bar, 1892; practised at Barbados bar; elec. to H. of A., Barbados, 1904-1913; mem., exec. comtes., 1909-1913; pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1913; asst. judge, et. of appeal, Barbados, 1921.

JACKS, PHILIP.—B. 1877; ed. Plymouth Coll.; admtd. solr., sup. ct., England, 1900; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1905; J.P., 1905; comsrr. for oaths, 1905; ag. land offr., 1907 to 1908; ag. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1908; ag. land offr., 1911; and from 1913-1914; land offr., 1916; ag. crown solr., 1919 and 1920; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1922; ag. puisne judge, 1927-28 and 1928-29.

JACKSON, EDWARD ST. JOHN, O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Stonyhurst and Beaumont Colleges and Brasenose College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, Oct., 1912; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Oct., 1918; judge of high ct., July, 1920; pres. of lands commnr., 1920; pres., finance commr., 1923; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1924; atty.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1929.

JACKSON, MAJOR FRANCIS WALTER FITTON, C.M.G. (1928), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. Dover Coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., 30th July, 1904; dist. commr., Northern Territories, G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1907; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 10th Mar., 1911; provincial commr., G. Coast, 23rd Jan., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., 1915, to Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1916, to June, 1918; ment. in desps. 3 times; O.C. British forces, and senr. polit. offr., Togoland, July, 1919; represented govt., in final handing over of Lome and that portion of Togoland ceded to France by the Franco-British decrn. of 10th July, 1919; dep. ch. commr., Ashanti, 1928.
JACKSON, GILBERT.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Sch., Guildford and Westminster; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 12th Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, ROBERT BEST, M.B., D.P.H., T.C.D., L.M. Rotunda Hospital, Dublin, certif. London Sch. Trop. Med., math. sizar, schl., and junr. modr., Trinity Coll., Dublin, senr. exhr. and catechetical prizeman.—Chief med. offr., Indian Mining Assn., Jharia and Katras Coalfields, 1908-14; R.A.M.C., 1914-17; med. offr., Gen. Milly Hosp., Edmonton, 1918; med. offr., F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; ag. health offr., Negri Sembilan, 1921.
JACKSON, T. — Curator, botanic station, Antigua, Leeward Is., 1905.

JACKSON, WILFRED EDWARD FRANCIS, C.M.G.—B. 1883; ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (classical scholar), B.A. 1906; personal sec. to gov., Trinidad, 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and clk to exec. coun., Bahamas, Sept., 1906, to Oct., 1907; asst. col. offr., Uganda, Nov., 1907; ag. dist. commr., Toro, July, 1911, to Apr., 1912; dist. commr., Apr., 1912; ag. asst. chief sec., July-Dec., 1912; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. chief sec., Aug., 1913, to Feb., 1914. May to Nov., 1914, and from Apr., 1915; col. sec., Bermuda, 19th Feb., 1916; col. sec., Barbados, 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, 13th Feb., 1926; ag. gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1929.
JAGGER, HON. J. W.—M.L.A for Cape Town (cent.); min. for rly. and hrbrs., Union of S. Africa, 1921-24.

JAMAICA, LORD BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. G. C. DE CARTERET, M.A., D.D.—Curate of St. Dunstan's, Canterbury, 1889; Holy Trinity, Tulse Hill, London, 1894; St. Mary, Cheltenham, 1896; vicar of St. Paul, Southwark, 1897; Christ Church, East Greenwich, 1901; rural dean of Greenwich, 1913; asst. bishop of Jamaica, 1913; bishop of Jamaica, 1916.

JAMES, SIR FREDERICK SETON, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E.(1922), C.M.G.(1902).—B. 1870; ed. at Charterhouse and abroad; asst. dist. commr., Niger Coast Prot., 1896; in charge at Opobo and Akwete, 1896-7; with peaceful mission to Bende in 1897; trav. commr., 1897; serv. as polit. offr. in expdn. agt. Ekuris, Cross River, 1898; settled disputes in Qua country after cent. div. expdn., 1899; recd. thanks of S. of S. divanl. commr., 1901; intell. and polit. offr. with Aro field force, 1901-2 (desps. medal); recd. thanks of dir.-gen. of intell. and S. of S. in connection with map of Aro operations; ag. sec. and dep. high commr., 1905; with Kwale patrol, Nov., 1905 (clasp); prov. commr. and mem. of etc. and legis. couns. of S. Nigeria on amalgamation, 1906; ag. col. sec. from 29th Mar., 1907; dep. gov., Apr. and Sept., 1907; ag. col. sec. and deputy gov. on various occasions from 1908 to 1912; ag. gov., Feb. to Sept., 1912; appd. admstr. on amalgamation of N. and S. Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; col. sec., Straits Settlements, June, 1916; seconded for spl. services as food contr., S. Stilimts, and Malay States, Dec., 1918; O.A.G. S. Stilimts. and high commr., Malay State, Aug., 1919; col. sec. in addition, May, 1919; O.A.G., Apr., 1922; col. sec., Feb., 1920; ag. ch. sec., F.M.S., June-Oct., 1920; col. sec., May, 1921; gov., Windward Is., 23rd Feb., 1924; assumed govt., 26th July, 1924.

JAMES, GODFREY WARDEN.—B. 1888; ed. at St. Paul's schl., London (Junior and Senior Foundation Scholar) and Oriel Coll., Oxford (Cornwallis Exhibtn., 1908), honrs. in class. med., 1909, and lit. hum., 1911; B.A. 1920; M.A. 1921; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1923; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1914; has acted as dist. commr., Port Lokko sub-dist., Bomba sub-dist., and Sherbro dist., and as asst. col. sec. for various periods from 1914-19; dist. commr., May, 1920; dist. commr., headqrs. dist., Mar., 1922.
JAMES, HERBERT BASIL.—Ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibtioner), 2nd class math. med., 1908; 3rd class final honour schl. nat. science, 1910; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910.

JAMES, JOSEPH EDWARD, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schl. and Micco Coll., Antigua; ag. educnl-offr., Antigua, Nov., 1901; clk., educ. dept. and educnl. offr., Antigua, Apr., 1904; schl. instr., St. John's Training Schl., Antigua Feb., 1913, to June, 1917; sub-instr. schl., Antigua, Apr., 1914; sub-instr., schl., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1919; local instr., schl., Mar., 1927; fedl. instr., schl., July, 1928 to Jan., 1929.
JAMES, MARSHALL NICOL.—B. 1875; served under the condominium govt., New Hebrides until Mar., 1922; govt. agr., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, KT. B.A. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schl.; admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. conven., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C. 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A. 1904-1906.

JAMISON, ROBERT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.(Ireland), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1880; asst. med. offr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, 1910-13; med. offr., Swaziland, 1913; serv. S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; prin. med. offr., 1918.

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; ohmn., Saamwerk Unie van Natale Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to present exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; rep. Vryheid in parl., since 1921; foundation mem. of Suid-Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; elec. speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924; min. of native affrs., June, 1929.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet local division, Ceylon civ. ser., Sep., 1914; attached to Kandy kach., Sept., 1914; attached to Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag. Batticaloa, in addition, Aug., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1915; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Jan., 1917; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1925; addnl. dist. judge, Avissawella, n addn., Oct., 1927; asst. attimt. offr., Sept., 1928.
JANSZ, JONATHAN CHARLES.—B. 1871; cl. v., Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1928; extra office test., Galle kach., Sept., 1928.

JARDINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip, 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; ov't. sec. to ag. high commr., July to Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. asst. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admtn., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expdy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in desp.); senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1927-28; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; ag. gov't., Jan. to Mar., 1929; joint-editor "Handbook of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, GORDON MURRAY.—Ed. Marlborough Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; asst. dist. commt., Uganda, 1912; cov. commdr., 4th Batt. K.A.R., 1917-18; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1919; dist. mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ret. without taking up appt., 1920; dist. mag., Entebbe, Uganda, 1921.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; passed final exam. in Tamil, Apr., 1914; ag. asst. contr. of lab., Apr., 1914; passed exam. in law, July, 1914; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Nov., 1917; ag. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1929.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes schol., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty-gen., O.F.S.; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1921.
JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; addl. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office asst., Matale kach., Jan., 1928.

JEFFERY, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corre. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archaeology of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, Feb., 1921; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," "Cyprus under an English King," "Historic Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem, edn., new edition (1929) of "An attempt at a Bibliography of Cyprus."

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1886; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltshire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. alk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; app'td., under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. alk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land stimlt. selection board, 1919; asst. sec., E. Africa currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comttee. on governors' pensions, 1927-28.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. asst., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1902; 1st asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief asst., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1926 and 1929.

JEKYLL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR HEBBERT, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1846; lieut. R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comttee., 1878; sec. to the roy. comm. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comttee., 1886; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1886, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for Melbourne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comm. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JELF, ARTHUR SELBORNE, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exh.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admstve. and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; asst. gen. adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; ohmn. san. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 16th Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., gen. staff, S. Stiltns command; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comttee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., 1st June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Perak, Nov., 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, cls. Ia, Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malays opium comttee., Dec., 1923; ag. contr. lab., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1924; pres., estates health commn., Apr., 1924 (reed. thanks of S. of S.); col. sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov't., Oct., 1925 to Apr., 1926; and June to Nov., 1928; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927.

JELLIICOE, 1ST VISCOUNT, OF SCAPA (GREAT. 1918); SIR JOHN RUSHWORTH JELLIICOE, O.M. (1916), G.C.B. (1915), G.C.V.O. (1916).—B. 1859 entd. navy, 1872; served in Egyptian war, 1882 China expeditionary force, 1900; European war, 1914-16; battle of Jutland; naval ast. to controller, 1901-3; dir. of naval ordinance, 1905-7; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1906-7; 3rd sea lord of the Admiralty and controller of the navy, 1908-10; comdr.-in-chief of the Atlantic fleet, 1910-11; and of the Grand Fleet, 1914-16; 2nd sea lord of the Admiralty, 1912-14; 1st sea lord, 1916-17; admiral of the fleet, 1919; governor-general of New Zealand, 1920-1924; G.C. Legion of Honour, and Croix de Guerre (France); G.C. Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium); St. George 3 (Russia); G.C.R.S. with Pavlovna (Japan); G.C. Savoia (Italy).
JOHNSON, E. T.—Res. mag., E. Africa Prot., 1915; senr. res. mag., Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M.—B. 1873; asst. clk., post office, 11th Sept., 1914; ch. clk., 1st Jan., 1924; ag. postmtr., 16th May to 31st Oct., 1925; asst. postmtr., 26th June, 1926.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspr. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of accts. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties; is also a dep. comsnr. of the high comsnr.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regiar. of high comsnr.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas., and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., 25th Nov., 1909.

JOHNSON, CAPT. GEORGE BENJAMIN.—B. 1834; ed. Ryder's Schl., Devonport and Cheltenham Training Coll. (dipl. educn.); Leicester Regt. and gen. staff, 1913-19; res. of ofrns., T.A.; ag. vice-consul, Le Havre, 1920; headmr., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1920; ditto and inspr., schl., 1925; ag. dir., educn. in 1920-23, 1925-26 and 1928; oh. inspr. schl., 1928.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNNINGHAM.—B. 1875; 2nd clk., treas., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; 2nd clk. admstr.'s office and clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1901; treas. and compt., ctn., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; ag. admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; offl. mem., exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis and offl. mem., Fed. exec. coun.; censor, St. Kitts during the war; is a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and admstr. govt., Antigua, June, 1925; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, Feb., 1927.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT.—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Schl., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; asst. inspr. W. and M., 1914; asst. sec. for native affs., 23rd Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1862; speaker of the H. of R., C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-23.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; ment. in desps., 1917; transf. to depot, 111th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of milly. accts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. accts. offr., O.E.T. admin., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admin., 1st July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and advisory couns., June to Sept., 1923, June to Sept., 1924, Jan., Apr. and May, 1925, May to Sept., 1926 and Jan. to June, 1928; mem., comtce. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comtce. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926; mem., rly. bd., 1928; currency offr., Jan.-June, 1928.

JOHNSON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls., and St. Francis Xavier Coll., Antigonish, Nova Scotia; entd. local legis. of Nova Scotia, April, 1897, as repres. for Cape Breton Co., Nova Scotia; resig., 1900; elec. to H. of C., 1900; re-elec., 1904; dep. min. marine and fisheries of Canada, June, 1910.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist, S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOKE.—B. 1898; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTON, REGINALD FLEMING, C.M.G. (1928), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; M.A. Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901); Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prox. acc. Lord Rector's essay, Edin.; hons. in English lit., mod. hist. and constitutional law, Edin.; exhib., Magd. Coll., Oxford, 1894; ment., hon. causa Starhope Essay, 1898; B.A., 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of couns., 1899; pvt. sec. to gov., 1900-01; ag. asst. col. sec., 1900-04; J.P., June, 1902; sec. to gov't., Weihiwei, 1904; dist. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906; seconded for serv. as tutor to Manchu Emperor (ex-Emporer of China), 1919-26; recd. from Emperor button of highest off. grade and sable robe, 1922; warden, Summer Palace, Peking, 1924; brought Emperor into safety in legation quarter, Peking, Nov., 1924; sec., Br. China indemnity delegation, 1926; comsnr., Weihiwei, 1927.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNGAN.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1908; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cls. II., polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comsnr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922.

JOHNSTONE, BANNER CARRUTHERS, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Cantab.); passed R.G.S. survey course, 1908; asst. supt., surveys, Caylon, Oct., 1909; asst. collr. (afterwards asst. dist. comsnr.), Zanzi- bar Prot., Apr., 1913; ag. 1st asst. sec. and clk. of coun., Nov., 1914 to June, 1915; ch. land attltnd. offr., Jan., 1916; seconded for active serv. in German E. Africa, Aug., 1916; on mil. serv., France, Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Pemba, July, 1919 to Apr., 1920; dist. comsnr., Pemba, Nov., 1921; ag. senr. comsnr., Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1920; ag. senr. comsnr. from Oct., 1926 to Apr., 1927; mem., leg. coun.; prov. comsnr., 1929.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGUE CHOLMELEY.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. asst. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; asst. supt., 1927.
JOHNSTONE, JOHN WILKIE.—B. 1884; solr., England, 1906; asst. polit. offr., G.E.A. (Tanganyika Territory), Nov., 1918; ag. mag., Apr., 1919 to Feb., 1921; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924.

JOHNSTONE, SIR ROBERT STEWART, KT. BACH. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary coman., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas, Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JONES, CHARLES BENJAMIN.—B. 1882; 6th cls. clk., treas. dept., S. Nigeria, 1905; 5th cls. clk., 1906; 4th cls. clk., div. commr.'s off., Bonny, 1906; prov. commr.'s off., E. Prov., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., Jan., 1907; head clk., sol.-gen.'s off., E. Prov., Sept., 1909; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1912.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mily. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment commn., Jan., 1922; addnl. astt. dir., educ., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Ellya, Apr., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925; astt. col. treas., Sept., 1928; astt. govt. agt., Matala, June, 1929.

JONES, G. A.—B. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; astt. sec. to Overseas Prize Disposal Comtee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1918; promoted, under O.-in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYM ARTHUR.—Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1909; astt. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; astt. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and director of the Victoria museum, 1915.
JONES, LLANELYN A. F.—Astt. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; junr. astt. sec., Feb., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., 1915; dist. commr., June, 1916; ag. seur. commr., Kenya, 1926 and 1928.

JONES, MALCOLM LUDLOW, O.B.E. (1919).—Served at Bd. of Trade (Labour Dept.), 1897-1901; chief clk., Emigrants' Information Office, 1901; visited Canada on behalf of E.I.O., 1907; chief clk., Oversea Setttm. Office, 1919.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYMAN.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cape, 1898; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1924; edited "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly with H. O. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S.A."; judge, Aug., 1926.
JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regtr., 1903; chief judge, native land ct., 1919; under sec., native dept., 1922.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.)—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Schl.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. hon. hist. (1908); cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. astt. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1914; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1916; astt. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917, astt. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Aug., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sep., 1923; local examn. in hv and Malay, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1923; astt. to federal secretariat on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; local examn. in Malay, Salang, Sept., 1924; ag. commr., trade and cust., Jehor, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1928.
JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; astt. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 26th Mar., 1913; dist. commr., 18th Dec., 1918; dep. prov. commr., 3rd Jan., 1924.

JONES, W. PATRICK.—Impl. service, 1891 to 1902; clk., treas., Transvaal, 18th Mar., 1902; priv. sec. to col. treas., Apr., 1903; principal clk., treas., 1st July, 1904; principal clk., dept. of finance, U. of S. Africa, 1910; chief clk., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1st Aug., 1912; chief clk. and acctnt., in rev. dept., Mar., 1917; ch. surr. of rev., 1st Nov., 1921; recr. rev., Johannesburg, Dec., 1923.

JONES-BATEMAN, REGINALD.—B. 1894; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Dec., 1919; office astt. to supt. of census and dir. of statistics, Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1924; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1924; astt. govt. agt., Kandy, Dec., 1926; astt. dist. offr., Aug., 1927.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhibr.)—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 28th Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 26th Apr., 1916; ag. astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 6th June, 1916; 2nd lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., 6th Dec., 1916; 1st lieut., F.M.S. govt. exam. in law, and passed cadet, 20th Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V, offgtg. as astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 26th Dec., 1916; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, 30th Nov., 1917; astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 15th Jan., 1918; lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., 6th Dec., 1918; astt. dist. offr., Ract (Bentong), 28th Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV, 28th Nov., 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan, 15th Apr., 1921; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1923; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; ag. astt. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Mar., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, July, 1929.
JORDAN, A. H. B.—1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOY, GEORGE ANDREW.—B. 1896; ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges; ent. Army, 1914; served in Belgium and France, 1915 and 1916; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; staff gen. headqu. Egypt and Palestine, 1919-20; S.W. Arabia, 1921 to 1923; ment. in despa., lieut., R.A.R.O., Feb., 1919; astt. to res. commr., New Hebrides, Aug., 1924; dep. commr., W. Pacific, 8th July, 1926; ag. Br. res. commr., 18th Aug., 1927; res. commr., New Hebrides, 1928.
JOYCE, G. F. W. C.—B. 1897; ent. R.L.O., June, 1912; astt. clk., Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., O.S.O., 1st Jan., 1920.

JOYCE, BREVIET LIEUT.-COL. PIERCE CHARLES, O.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 1st Batt. The Connaught Rangers, 1900; serv. S. African War, 1900-02 (severely wounded, Queen's Med. with 3 clasps, King's Med. with 2 clasps); attd., Egyptian Army, 1907-16 (Egyptian Med. with clasp, 4th
