ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and City and Guilds Engng. Coll., S. Kensington; held various engnr. appts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1908; asst. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag.dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1922.

ROPER, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1891; entd. C.A. office, 1907; seconded to Falkland Is., Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to admistr. and clk. to couns., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; ag. local audr., Nov., 1914; retnd. to C.A. office, 1915; served European war (M.G.C., France), 1916-1919; 1st cls. clk., Nyasaland, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. clk., July to Dec., 1921; edr., Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., in 1922, 1925 and 1926; organising sec., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Sept., 1926; ag. ch. transport offr., Dec., 1926 to May, 1927; office supt., secretaries, Apr., 1927; sec., govt. tender bd., Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. staff offr., Nyasaaland Vol. Res., Apr. to Nov., 1929; ag. senr. asst. sec., May, 1929.

ROSE, HON. HUGH EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. at Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto; (B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Fasken, Cowan, Chadwick and Rose; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, Dec., 1916; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.

ROSS, ALEXANDER HOWARD, C.B.E. (1929).—B. 1880; ed. Epsom Coll. and on H.M.S. "Worcester"; supervisor of cuat., Gold Coast, 1905; ag. inspr., P.S., 1908 and 1909; ag. dist. commr. and travelling commr., 1908 and 1909 in Ashanti and N. Territories; asst. dist. commr., Ashanti, 1910; ag. prov. commr., 1912 and 1913; ag. dist. commr., 1913; dist. commr., 1914; ag. prov. commr., Mar. to Oct., 1916; passed in criminal law, Trop. African Service Course; dist. commr., 1917-18; ag. prov. commr., Mar. to Sept., 1919; ag. sec., native affrs., May to June, 1919; prov. commr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920.

ROSS, GEORGE ARCHIBALD PARK, M.D., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.); diploma, trop. diseases, Liverpool.—B. 1879; S. African War, Lovat's Scouts, 1900-02; Anglo-Portuguese bndry. comm., Zambesia, 1904-5; native rebellion, Natal, 1906; trop. diseases investigtn., 1906-10; govt. pathologist, Natal, 1911; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, statnd. in Natal, 1918; major, S. African med. corps, in ch. sleeping sickness bureau, Durban, and sanitation offr., Natal, 1914-19.

ROSS, J. G. H.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Sept., 1919; ag. junr. asst. sec., Kenya, Nov., 1919 to Oct., 1919; ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1919 to Mar., 1920; editor, offl. Gazette, Feb., 1920 to Mar., 1920.

ROSS, JAMES PAUL.—B. 1881; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A.; writer to the Signet; capt., 5th Batt. The Royal Scots (Q.R.R.); declined to retain rank of capt. as permission to rejoin for war serv. refused; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 13th Aug., 1913; ag. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1918; dist. commr., 9th Apr., 1919; ag. pol. mag., Kumasi, 12th Apr., 1919 to 5th Feb., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec., 29th July to 31st Dec., 1920; ch. census offr. in addn., 1921; asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. ch. registr. and sheriff, 16th July, 1921 to 22nd Oct., 1921; senr. asst. col. sec., 15th Nov., 1921; pres., Accra town coun., 1921, 1923, 1924, 1925, 1926 and 1927; commr. for munic. affrs., 1st Apr., 1926.
ROSS, JAMES SANDFORD.—B. 1884; ed. Merchant Taylors Sch.; mily. serv., 1914-19; N.W. Frontier campaign, 1915; Salonica Field Force, 1916-19; asst. treas., Kenya, 1919.

ROSS, OSWALD F.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; capt., N. Staffs. Regt., European War; served Dardanelles expdn., 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916; passed A.T.S. course, 1920; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1920.

ROSS, CAPT. WILLIAM ALSTON, C.M.G. (1922).—B. 1875; dist. commr., Nigeria, 1903; dist. commr., 1st grade, S. Nigeria, 1910; senr. dist. commr., 1912; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, 1914-16; staff grade, 1920.

ROSSOUW, GEORGE STEFANUS HAUPFTLEISCH, B.A. (Cape), M.A. and Ph.D. (Chicago).—B. 1896; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Chicago Univ., U.S.A.; senr. lecturer, sociology, Univ. of Minnesota, U.S.A., 1921-22; ethnologist, Transvaal Museum and part-time lect., social anthropology, Trans. Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1921; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1926.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII's schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1999; ag. Crown pros., Feb., 1900; Crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mast. and registr., sup. ct., 1st Jan., 1927; ag. puisne judge, Aug., 1929.

ROUSE, HAROLD STUART, O.B., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Truro Coll. and Christ's Hosp.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; engnr. drainage wks. office, Dec., 1913; transf'd. to gen. wks. office, P.W.D., Apr., 1915; ag. exec. engnr., G.W.O., Sept., 1915-16 and from Feb., 1921 to Oct., 1922; offr. in charge, G.W.O., May, 1924; sec., water emergency comm., May to Aug., 1929.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAMY.—B. 1856; ed. St. Mary's Hall, Southsea, and Cadet school; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; asst. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; ag. marine supt., Dec., 1903, to June, 1904; comdr., H.M.C.S.Y., "Ivy," S. Nigeria, June, 1906; sen. marine offr., E. Prov., S. Nigeria, May, 1907.

ROWATT, HUGH HOWARD.—B. 1861; ed. Ottawa Coll. Inst., and St. Catherine's Affiliated Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1887, as correspondent; ch. clk., mining lands bd., Mar., 1907; contrl. mining lands, 1912; supt., 1920; mem., Dominica lands bd., 1928.

ROWE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ent. Crown Agent's office, 1896; asst. head, stores dept., 1917; asst. head, pay dept., 1920; cashier, 1921; dep. head, finance dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1929.

ROWE, Lieut.-Col. R. H., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Wellington Coll. and Royal Milly. Acad., Woolwich; Royal Art'y., 1902-22; Bermuda and Jamaica, 1902-6; served with native infantry, Nigeria, 1907-10; Northern Hinterland exped., 1907-8; Niger Cross River exped., 1908-9; operations against Abini, 1909, and against Munchu, 1910; dep. dir., surveys, Nigeria, 1911; Nigeria-Dahomey bndry. commr., 1912; Great war (Cameroon, France and Russia), 1914-18; survr-gen., Gold Coast, 1920; commr., lands, in addn., 1925; Gold Coast rep. with staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to W. Africa, 1925; chmn. comtees. on pub. wks. acts, rly. constrn., munic. finance and mily. defence; mem., leg. coun., 1926; commr., lands, Nigeria, 27th Oct., 1926.

ROWELL, THOMAS HUBERT.—Clk., loco. dept., I.M.R., Pretoria, Oct., 1900; acctg. brch. ch. loco. supt.'s office, Jan., 1903; acctg. offr., ch. mech. engnr.'s dept., July, 1908; inspr., audit dept., S.A.R. and Harbors., Oct., 1910; inspr., asst. contr. and audr. gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, May, 1911; inspr., (rlys. and hbrs.) control and audit dept., 1st July, 1916; senr. inspr., 1st Oct., 1922; ag. ch. inspr., rlys. and harbrs., 24th Apr. to 11th Sept., 1924.

ROWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912; 3rd cls. clk., asty. gen.'s off., 1915; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1918; clk. to regsr.-gen., 1919; sec., riot enquiry comm., 1919; ag. regsr.-gen. and cust. enemy ppty. and and contr. local clearing office, 1920; sec., yellow fever enquiry comm., 1921; again ag. reg. gen., 1921, 1923 and 1926; reg. gen., reg. lands titles, reg. sup., vice-adm. and bank, ots., reg., companies and patents and reg., births marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp coms., dir., pub. offrs. guarantee fund; coms., income tax, 1927.

ROWLEY, FORTESCUE WILLIAM, I.S.O. (1929).—B. 1874; ed. Canterbury Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. dept. of lab., 1893; dep. chief inspr. of factories, 1907; chief clk. and dep. chief inspr., 1911; sec., of lab., ch. inspr. of factories, reg. of industrial unions, 1914.

RUDDICK, JOHN ARCHIBALD, LL.D.—B.1862, dairy expert; astt. to dairy coms., Canada, 1891; prin. dairy schl., Kingston, Ont., 1896; dairy coms., New Zealand, 1898; astt. to coms., agr., Can., 1900; dairy coms., Can., 1905; dairy and cold storage coms., Can., 1906; hon. LL.D. (Queen's Univ.), 1924.

RUGGLES, NEPEAN CLARK, K.C., LL.B.—Barrister of sup. ct., Nova Scotia; Bachelor of Laws at Dalhousie Coll., Halifax, Canada, with honours, 1891; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, with highest honours, 1891; K.C., Nova Scotia, Jan., 1911, served with the Canadian forces during S. African War; now on the reserve of officers, Canadian militia; public prosecutor at Barberton, Transvaal, 1902; astt. res. mag., 1903 to 1907; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Oct., 1909 to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., Roseau and reg., sup. ot. in addition on various occasions; took census of Dominica, 1911; mem., leg. coun., Dominica; mag., Grenada, 1915 to 1920; chmn., several coms., while in Grenada; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just. for short period; 1st cls. stip. mag., B. Guiana, Apr., 1921.

RUNDLE, GEN. HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1856; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1896; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. R.A., 1876; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Zulu war, 1879; Boer war, 1881; Egyptian war, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-5; Soudan Frontier field force, 1885-7; Soudan Frontier, 1889; Dongola expdn., 1896; adjt.-gen., Egyptian army; Khartoum expdn., 1898; comdg. S.E. Dist., 1898-9; dep. adjt.-gen. to the forces, 1899-1900; in cmd. of div., Aldershot, 1900; lieut.-gen. on staff in cmd. of 8th Div., S. African field force, 1900, 1902; lieut.-gen. of Nthn. Comd., 1905-7; col.-cmdt., R.A., 1907; gov. and comdr.-in-chief Malta, 1909-1915.

RUSEMER, JAMES.—B. 1876; entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896; promoted, after exam., under order-in-council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., 18th Feb., 1911; astt. sec. to colonial blue-books comtee., 1917-1918; seconded for serv. at overseas settlement office, Apr., 1920.

RUSTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—Ck., coms., rly. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to coms. of rly., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901; sec., W. Australian London Agency, 3rd Nov., 1919.

RUSHTON, HERBERT HARVEY.—B.1876; acot., electric light dept., Gibraltar, 1897; astt. sec. and astt. acot., munic. authority, 1902; ch. acot. and astt. sec., 1903; sec. and finan. offr., 1908 (also man., pub. electricity and water supplies, mem., comte., on exchange and currency); astt. ch. acot., Uganda Rly., E. Africa Prot., 1915; lieut., E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., 1916; ag. dep. ch. acot., Uganda Rly., 1916 to June, 919 (also govt. rep., Nairobi munici. coun., 1918, mem., gen. manager's advisory comtee., 1919); ag. ch. acot. to Uganda Rly., June to Dec., 1919; dep. treas., Kenya Colony, 1920 and 1921; ag. treas., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. couns. (also chmn., currency and other comtees.); col. treas., Fiji, 1922 (with charge of group of depts., cust., port and marine, transport, posts, telegraphs and telephones, govt. stores, and returned soldiers' dairy stltnt.); mem., exec. and leg. couns.; coms., of currency, mem., educ., mining and other bds.; chmn., many comtees.; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1929; gov.'s dep. for four periods.

RUSSELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ANDREW HAMILTON.—K.C.B. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1868; held coman. as lieut. for 5 yrs. in Imperial Army; vol. offr., New Zealand, 1900; hon. A.D.C. to H.E. the Gov. of N.Z., 1911; brig.-gen., 1914; temp. major-gen., 1915; major-gen. in command of N.Z. Div., 1916; foreign decoration, Order of Danilo (Montenegro), 1917.

RUSSELL, HON. BENJAMIN, K.C., M.A., D.C.L.—Ed. at Halifax gram. schl. and Mount Allison Univ.; called to the bar, 1872; for some years law adviser to legis. coun., Nova Scotia; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1896; judge of supreme ct., Nova Scotia, 1904.

RUSSELLI, SIR WILLIAM ALISON, KT. BACH (1928), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., LL.B., honours, 1897; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1900; equity bar, 1900-6; apptd. Crown advoc., pub. proscr. admstr.gen., prin. reg. of docmts., reg. of companies, Uganda Prot., May, 1906; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Jan., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Aug., 1909, to Mar., 1910; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Mar. to May, 1910; ag. chief sec. to govt., June, 1910 to May, 1911; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., June, 1911 to June, 1912; King's advoc., Cyprus, July, 1912; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1912; joint coman. under Reprint of Statutes Law, 1913; edition pubd., Feb., 1914; K.C., Cyprus, 1924; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; published "Legislative and other Forms" (2nd edn. 1928) and "Handbook for Magistrates" (2nd edn. 1928).
RUSSELL, WILLIAM HAYWORTH.—B. 1894; clk., Mankaiana, Swaziland, Apr., 1915; clk., treasury, 1920; astt. acct. and astt. collr., inc. tax, 1923; acot., 1928.

RUTHERFORD, GEORGE JAMES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. Lond.—Ed. at Epson Coll. and Middlesex Hosp.; astt. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; on spec. serv. in Lagos during the Sarki expdn., 1897-98; sen. med. offr., G. Coast, 1907; sen. med. officer, S. Nigeria, 1907; prov. med. offr., Ashanti, 1910; astt. prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr. gen. of hospitals, Ceylon, 1911; Ceylon deleg. at "All India" sanitary confere., 1911-12; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Ceylon, Oct. to Dec., 1912, Feb. to Dec., 1913, and from June to Aug., 1915; prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Aug., 1915; M.L.C.; sec., col. advisory med. and san. comte., at C.O., 1924.

RUTHERFORD, CAPT. PETER WILLIAM, M.C. (1916), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; 2nd lieut., W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1916; seconded, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; retd. from army, 1919; astt. dist. coman., G. Coast, 20th Sept., 1921.
RYRIE, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR GRANVILLE DE LAUNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), O.M.G.
SALOMONE, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. St. Ignatius Coll. and Malta Univ.; elec. for 6th divn. in leg. assm., Oct., 1921; re-elec., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for industry and commerce, 16th Aug., 1927.
SAMARAKOON, EDWIN RICHARD DE ALVEIS.—B. 1877; cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1927; extra office astt., Badulla kach., Oct., 1927; office astt. to contr., rev., Feb., 1928.
SAMBIDGE, GEORGE CHURCHILL.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Sch., Birmingham; served Nigeria Regt., 27th Mar., 1915; instr. in arts and crafts, N. Prova., Nigeria, 1st Dec., 1920.

SAMPSON, HON. HENRY WILLIAM—B. 1872; mem., Transvaal parlmt., 1907-10; mem., Union parlmt., 1910-29; O.B.E., 1918; joined Union cabinet as min. for posts and tele. and pub. wks., Nov., 1928; pres., S.A. typographical union, 1903-29.

SAMUEL, Rt. Hon. Sir HERBERT LOUIS, P.C. (1908), G.B.E. (1920), M.A.—B. 1870; ed. University Coll. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P., Cleveland Divn., N. Riding, Yorks., 1902-18; parly. under sec., home dept., 1905-9; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster (with a seat in the cabinet), 1904-10 and 1915-16; P.M.G., 1910-14 and 1915-16; pres. of the L.G.B., 1914-15; sec. of state for home affairs, 1916; chmn. of the select comte. of house of commons on national expenditure, 1917-18; British sp. commr. to Belgium, 1919; grand off. of the Order of Leopold of Belgium, 1919; high commr., Palestine, 1920-26.

SAMUEL, SILVERIO I., M.B.E. (1924).—Ed. at St. Gregory's Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; messr. and copyt., educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to instr. of schls., Mar., 1899; sec. to comte. for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. commr., Ilesha, interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., gov't. off., Aug., 1904; conf. clk. to the gov't. of Nigeria.

SAMUELS, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1876; ed. Chesterfield Sch. Birr., King's Co., Ireland, Corrig Sch., Kingstown and Trinity Coll., Dublin; med. offr. and mag., Anguilla, B.W. Indies, Apr., 1909; med. supt., cent. mental hosp., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; publications, "General paralyses of the Insane in the F.M.S." (Jnl. of Mental Science, Apr., 1916); "A case of Pellagra in the Cent. Asylum, Tanjong Rambutan, F.M.S." (Jnl. of Mental Science, Oct., 1917); "Malaria and Insanity" (Transactions of the Far Eastern Assn. of Trop. Med., 1923); "A History of the Central Mental Hosp., Tanjong Rambutan" (Jnl. of Mental Science, Jan., 1926).

SANDBERG, JOHN OWEN, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Elstow Sch. and Manchester Univ.; mil. ser., 1915-20; 2nd lieut., 1917; ag. capt., 1918; asst., Crewe loco. works, L.M.S.R., 1922; works man., F.M.S. Rly., 1924; run. supt., 1927.
SANDERSON, G. M.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., med. offr., Nyasa land, 1910; temp. major, Nyasa land Field Force, Aug., 1914; sanitation offr., Nov., 1921; ag. D.M.S.S., 1927.

SANDFORD, GEORGE RITCHIE, O.B.E. (1924), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1924.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; priv. sec. to gov', 1918; junr. astt. sec. and clk. of coun., Sept., 1922; ag. sec. astt. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1923; resig., 1925; clk. to leg. coun., Jan., 1926; edr., official gazette, Mar., 1926.

SANDYS, MELVILL KEVERNE TREWANTRY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; attd., Ratnapura kach., Nov., 1919; office astt. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Putalam, Oct., to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, July, 1923; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Oct., 1926; ag. agt., Hambantota, May, 1927; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, June, 1927; asst. govt. agt., Uva, July, 1927; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matara, Aug., 1927; asst. settlmt. officer, Jan., 1929.

SANGER-DAVIES, ARTHUR ELPHINSTONE.—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll., Coopers Hill and Oxford (Diploma of Forestry, 1907); asst. conservator, forests, grade II., F.M.S. forest dept., 1907; grade I., 1912; dep. conservator, forests, 1919; ag. conserv., forests, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1923; dep. conserv., forests, Oct., 1923.

SANSON, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamed Coll.; Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. probationer, 1903; ag. asst. commr. of pol., 1908; ag. registrar of criminals, Selangor, 1909; registrar of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; asst. commr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913-20; commr. of pol., Johore, 1921-24; ag. commr. of pol., F.M.S., 1924.

SARAVANAMUTTU, P.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Colombou, Kach., Aug., 1919; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Kalutara, Feb., 1920; addnl. to Matale Kach., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, Mar., 1922; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923; addnl. asst. dir., educ., Dec., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1929.
SARGENT, CAPT. R. G., M.B.E. (1928), A.I.N.A., F.R.G.S.—Port. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917; port capt., 1925.
SARGENT, JOHN DENYS, B.A. (Oxon), Dipl., Forestry, Oxford.—B. 1886; asst. conserv., forests, Ceylon, Dec., 1909; dep. conserv., grade II., Apr., 1917; grade I., May, 1919; conserv., Sept., 1921.

SAUNDERS, Sir CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906, O.M.G., 1896.—B. 1837; held various appts. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1888-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. commr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. commr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. commr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Comsn., 1902, and commr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, JOHN C.—B. 1862; ed. pub. schls. and High Schl., Halifax, N.S.; Acadia Univ., Wolfville, N.S.; ent. pub. serv., Mar., 1882; transferred to finance dept., Ottawa, July, 1887; asst. dep. min. of finance, Jan., 1917; dep. min. of finance, Apr., 1920.

SAUNDERS-JONES, DAVID WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1899; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon; Indian Army, 1917-23; on service in Waziristan, Egypt and Palestine; wounded in Palestine, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; prte. sec., Br. res., June to Dec., 1925.

SAVAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; supt. of registration, G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; supt. of mails, ditto, Jan., 1915; ag. supt. of mails, Penang, Jan., 1915; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Feb., 1917; supt. of mails, Penang, but continued ag. senr. supt. of mails, July, 1917; supt., grade II., Jan., 1919; ag. supt., money order branch and savings dept., May, 1919 and Nov., 1920; supt., grade I., but cont. to act as above, Jan., 1920; supt., posts and telegraphs, Malacca, Sept., 1920; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank dept., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. P.M.G., Singapore, Apr., 1921; ag. acctnt., G.P.O., Singapore, July, 1921 to Nov., 1921; ag. asst. P.M.G., July, 1922.

SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; registr. and marshal and registr.-gen. and prin. registr., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925 and 1926; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1928.

SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—Pol. probationer, S. Stltima., Nov., 1904; asst. supt. of pol., Jan., 1908; passed final exam. in Punjabi, Feb., 1910; ag. 2nd supt. of pol., Aug., 1910 and Feb., 1914; ag. supt. of pol., Dec. to Feb., 1914; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Sept., 1916; asst. supt. of pol., Penang, Oct., 1917; ag. dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1919; dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1920; ag. supt., pol. and oh. pol. offr., Singapore, May, 1921; dep. commr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1922; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak in addn., July, 1926; ag. commr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1925 to Jan., 1926.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CARMION, M.C.(1916), M.M.(1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-I) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; hom. serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; comand., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps., Mar., 1919; surrv., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1925-28; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927.

SAWREY-COOKSON, SYDNEY SPENCER, R.A.—B. 1876; ed. Uppingham and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1903; judicial commr., N. Borneo, 1910-20; compiler, revd. edn., N. Borneo laws; judge, sup.ct. Gambia, 1920; compiler, revd. edn. (1926) Gambia laws; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 11th June, 1926.

SAWTELL, WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. at King Edward VI's Sch., Bath, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; H.M. Transport Serv., 1916-17; Indian govt. tel. ship, 1917-18; Indian Army R. of O., 1918-22; wharf-mast. and pilot, Tanganika Territory, 1922.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1905; paymr.-lieut.-comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptnt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. commr., Accra, and ag. trav. commr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; promoted pay commdr., ret. list, 1927.

SAYER, GEOFFREY ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (founder) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhibr. in classics and Fitzgerald exhibr.; 2nd cls. class. moda.; 3rd cls. lit. hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. ast. dist. offr. (South), 1912 to 1913; ag. ast. supt. pol., 1913 to 1914; ag. dep. offr. recvr., 1914 to 1915; ag. ch. ast. sec. for Chinese affra. 1914 and 1915, in addn. to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addn. 1915; astd. to crown solr.'s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1916; ag. chief ast. sec. for Chinese affra., 1915; on milly. serv., 1915-19; dep. regis. and appraiser, Hong Kong, 1920; head, sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921; astd. to treasy. 1924; ast. treas. and comsmr. of estate duties, Apr. 1925; supt., impts. and expts. dept., June, 1927; P.M.G., Apr., 1928; head, sany. dept., 1928.

SAYERS, ELDERD FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; ast. dist. comsmr., Sierra Leone, 1st Jan., 1915; dist. comsmr., 21st Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, FREDERICK RICHARDSON—M.D., D.P.H., Dublin Univ.; ed. Rathmines Schl., Dublin, The Meath Hosp., Dublin, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; res. med. offr., Monkstown Hosp., Dublin, 1909-12; med. offr., S. Settmts., 23rd Mar., 1912; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, 27th Apr., 1912; med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, 2nd June, 1912; port health offr. and health offr., rural areas, Penang, cor., Penang, and med. offr. in charge of leper asylum, Pulau Jerejak, Penang, 3rd Sept., 1912; ag. ast. port health offr., Singapore, 10th Sept., 1912; resumed duties in Penang, 21st Oct., 1912; ast. censor, Penang, in addition, Sept., 1915, to Apr., 1916; ag. sen. health offr., S. Settmts., in addition, Mar., 1917; ag. ast. health offr., rural areas, Singapore, Mar., 1919; ag. chief health offr., S. Stltmts., in addition, Apl., 1919; lecturer in hygiene, King Edward VII. medical schl., Singapore, June, 1919; ag. ch. health offr., Singapore, in 1920, 1923, 1926, 1926 and 1928; senr. health offr., Penang, Feb., 1924; promoted cls. B., Jan., 1925; mom., Br. delegation to internat. sany. confce. Paris, May to June, 1926; senr. health offr., Perak, Feb., 1927; ch. health offr., S'pore, May, 1928.

SAYERS, GERALD FLeming.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; ast. dist. comsmr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. ast. sec. Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. sec., Tanganyika territory, 21st July, 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923; astd. to O.O., Aug., 1924 to Sept., 1925; clk., leg. coun., 1926; ag. ast. ch. sec., Apr., June, 1927.

SAYLE, Lieut. Commdr., ARTHUR REGINALD WILLES, R.N.R., Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—Ed. Merchant Taylors Schl. and cadet H.M.S. "Worcester"; Royal Navy, May, 1914; R.I.C., 1920; admstrv. offr., S. Nigeria, May, 1921; station mag., Ibadan, S. Nigeria, Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Oct., 1928.
SCADDAN, Hon. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organising sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; ast. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hoops. coman., Canada, 1915; ast. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-establish., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law elk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; ast. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examnr., May, 1898; ch. examnr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. elk., Apr., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, Sept., 1926; contr. and audr.-gen., 1929.
SCHNEIDER, Sir Gualterus Stewart, Kt.Bach. (1928), K.C.—B. 1864; ag. puisne just., Ceylon, Jan., 1921; comsmr. of assize, Nov., 1921; puisne just., Dec., 1921; ag. ch. just., Sept., Dec., 1926.

SCHOCH, August Arnold.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional ast. to atty.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dists., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman; dept. of just., Pretoria, Sept., 1925.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, 17th Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; ast. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, 12th Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; ag. 2nd ditto, 1915; passed law exam., 1916; seconded to H. Kong and Shanghai Bank, 1918; ag. 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1918 to 1919, ag. dep. regis. and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919; ag. 2nd ast. to ditto, 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. ch. ast., Aug., 1920 to Nov., 1921; dist. offr., South in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923; mag., Hong Kong, 1926; mag., Kowloon, 1927.

SCHOLEFIELD, Guy Hardy, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly liby. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILLIAM.—Dep. elk., res. mag.'s ct., Jamaica, 12th Mar., 1913; elk., res. mag.'s ct., 26th Feb., 1917; ast. comsmr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, 19th July, 1919; ag. comsmr., ditto, 1st Sept. to 12th Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCICLUNA, Hannibal P., L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regisr., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigrn. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; librn., Malta public library, July, 1923.

SCIORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyceum Univ., Malta; in service of Eastern and S. African telegraph companies, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1903; ast. res., 1904; 3rd cl. res., 29th Apr., 1907; 2nd cl. res., 15th June, 1910; senr. res., 12th June, 1921.

SCOTLAND, Douglas William, F.S.I., N.D.A., N.D.D., H.A.A.C. Dip.—Ed. at Musoroe Schi., India and Alleynes Schi., Stone, Staffs.; Agric. Coll., Newport, Salop, 1904-1907; Salop County Scholar, 1906-1907; Profanl. Assoc. of Survrs. Inst., 1909; agric. instr., Tyrone (Board of Agric. Ireland), 1907-1908; lectr. at Harper-Adams agric. coll., 1908-11; astt., agric. dept., Sierra Leone, 1911-1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions; dir. of agric., S. Leone, Dec., 1915; visited W. Inds. and Br. Guiana on behalf of S. Leone govt., Nov., 1913 to Feb., 1914; del. to oil palm conf., at C.O., Sept., 1919; ag. commr., lands and forests, and M.L.C. in 1924, 1926 and 1927; del. to 2nd Imp. Entomological Conf., 9th to 18th June, 1925.
SCOTLAND, Erica Eleanor.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, Duncan Campbell, F.R.S.C.—B. 1862; apptd. to Indian commr.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. elk., dept. of Indian affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; 1st cl., 1889; ch. olkship, 1893; ch. acctnt. and supt. of Indian educn., 1908; deputy supt. gen. of Indian affairs, 1913; hon. sec., Royal Soc. of Canada.

SCOTT, Eustace Lindsay, O.B.E. (1918), M.C. (1917).—B. 1885; ed. Bradford, and Univ. Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1908; astt. dist. commr., Uganda, Dec., 1908; dist. commr., June, 1918; seconded for milly. serv., 1914-19; dep. astt. dir. transport, 1916; dep. dir. milly. lab., E.A.E.F., 1917; lab. commr., 1921; astt. sec. for native affrs. Jan., 1922; astt. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1924 to Jan., 1925; ag. dep. ch. sec. in 1927 and 1928.

SCOTT, Henry Harold, M.D., London Univ.; F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M., and H. (Camb.) Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosp.; Univ. Colls., London and Copenhagen; protector, 1894; astt. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1898; astt. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willensted Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII. mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge Hosp., Aldershot, 1916; govt. pathologist, dir., serum inst., Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect. trop. disease, Westminster Hosp., med. sch., 1928; examr., D.M.T. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research comte, 1928.

SCOTT, Herbert Septimus.—B. 1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educn. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1924; dir., educn., Kenya, 1928.

SCOTT, John, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admng. the govt., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1906; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910; ditto, Hambantota, May, 1911; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; astt. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addtl. astt. col. sec., Aug., 1914; attached to censor's off., Apr., 1915; censor, Sept., 1915; prin. astt. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; col. sec., S. Sttltns., 1928.
SCOTT, Margaret.—Ed. Sherbourne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in despa., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924.

SCOTT, Thomas.—Clk., aud. office, Transvaal, Feb., 1901; inspr., Jan., 1904; prin. clk., rev. div., treasry., Oct., 1904; rev. inspr., Mar., 1905; prin. clk., audit office, Dec., 1906; chief inspr. of expenditure audit, control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; ag. astt. contr. and audr. gen., Feb. to Aug., 1921 and May to Oct., 1922; astt. contr. and audr. gen., Oct., 1927.

SCOTT, Walter Richardson.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; astt. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1926; attd. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu, 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; prte. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C., to gov., 1929.
SCRAGG, John, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Maneseter Gram. Schi. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; schol., 2nd cls., philos., politics and economics, 1925; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

SCRIVENER, Rupert Frederick.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schi. of Minee, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc. Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; comsmd. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; astt. ch. engrnr., Palestine rlys., 1920; engrnr., ways and wks., Palestine rlys., 1924.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROOKE.—B. 1876; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. senr. warden, mines, F.M.S., in addn., Mar.-Sept., 1908; served in the Army in England and France from Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service; on sp. duty for state of Kelantan, May, 1924; dir., geological survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1927.
SCULLIN, JAMES HENRY.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1929.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. astt. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1922; ag. astt. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. astt. to res. commr., May, 1923 to Aug., 1924 and from 18th Aug., 1927; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) 1st Apr., 1925.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confidl. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treasy. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to commrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasy., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1896; clk. to sub-recev., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1904.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl. 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cl. hist. tripes, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme astt. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; astt. prin., C.O., 1922; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929.

SEDWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S. Stiltna; civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to aspt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supernny. offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. astt., treas. and commr. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; ed. St. John's (R-C) Coll., Wonesh, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; astt. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. astt. audr., July, Sept., 1926; astt. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. astt. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macsfield Grammar Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mili. serv., Cheshire Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher divn. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as astt. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; sec., managing comte., imp. bureau of entomology; pvt. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the Cols. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June, 1923.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (depts., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French gov't. for saving lives at sea, 1891; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comte., to inquire into organisation of crown agents' office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; 1st parly. sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; astt. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man.-sec., agric. bank, Jan., 1928.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.L.—B. 1886; astt. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, 1st Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., 1st Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., 1st June, 1917; gen. astt. to astt. D.P.W., 1st Jan., 1925.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., registr.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; agt. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1906). THE RT. HON. WM. WALDGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cl. hist.), 1882; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1886-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-96; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1896; chmn., Pacific cable comte., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comte., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transval and O.R.C., 1906 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.

SELBY, PHILIP HAMILTON.—B. 1870; B.S.A. Pol., 1890-91; collr., N. E. Rhodesia, 1st July, 1898; civ. commr. and mag., 1900; judge of administrator's ct., N.W.R., 1905; mag., N.R., 17th Aug., 1911; ag. sec. for mines on various occasions, 1921 to 1924; ag. sec. for agr. and mines, 4th Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun.; J.P.

SELDON, ARTHUR ARNOLD.—B. 1889; ed. Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A.(hon.); astt. dist. commr., E.A.F., Dec., 1913; cap., K.A.R., 1917-18; sec., famine comte., Nairobi, 1919; personal astt. to ch. native commr., 1920; 1st grade admstr. offr., 1926; H.M.'s ag. consul. S. Abyssinia, 1928.

SELIS, MARTIN PERONET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. asst. loco. supt., Lancs. and Yorks. Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rly., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in desps.); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engr., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1920; ch. mech. engr., Gold Coast rly., 1922; ch. mech. engr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SELWYN-CLARKE, PERCY SELWYN, M.C., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Eng.) with distinc., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—Ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health, W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. san. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., san. serv., May to Aug., 1927; del. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever confere., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; asst. dir., san. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Médaille d'Argent des Épidémies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1983; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., 1920; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1909; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regiar., Leeward Is., and regiar. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., regiar. of titles, regiar. of friendly societies, and regiar.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regiar. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regiar. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1906; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag. dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr., in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner, dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.
SEMPILL, CHARLES IGNATIUS, M.M.—B. 1825; Australian Forces, 1914-19; asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1920.

SEMPILL, J. HOWARD, M.B.E. (1925)—B. 1877; ed. Stirling High Sch.; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1914; ch. of pol. and provost marshal 1916; ditto and marshal, sup. ct. in admy., 1920; supt. pol., Br. Honduras, 1927.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1918; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929.

SENARATNE, CORNELIUS.—B. 1870; cl. v., Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; extra office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1921; office asst. to asst. gov't. agt., Matale, Feb., 1922; ditto, Ham-bantota, June, 1926.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mil'y. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B'Cala Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1922; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. gov't. agt., Ratnapura and addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalle, Jan., 1929.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd., Matara kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927; asst. stimlt. offr., Apr., 1929.

SENNETT, CEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Stiltnits, 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; asst. censor, 1916; asst. regist., impta. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolics, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921; supermny. offr., clv. IV., 28th Nov., 1921.
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief accont., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SERRET, EVARISTE JOSEPH EUGENE.—B. 1864; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; English school., 1884; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1887; crown pros., Mauritius, 1892; addnl. substitute procuror and advoc. gen., 1901; ag. ch. just., Seychelles, 1911-12; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 1913; ag. proc. and advoc. gen., 1913-14; K.C., 1919; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1922.

SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; asst. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to asst. atty.-gen., May, 1926; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, Laleham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mil'y. serv., 1914-19; M.C., twice ment. in desps.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926.

SEVERN, SIR CLAUD, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. olk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov', S. Stiltnits., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1896; passed in Malay law, 1898; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906 to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high commr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov', S. Stiltnits., May, 1907; sec. to high commr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; sec. cong., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; adminst. gov't. on various occasions, 1912-13, 1916-19, 1921-22; ret., 1926.

SEIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., county of Quebec; unsuccessful candidate, legis. assem., Quebec, bye-elec., 1907; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 3rd Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; asst. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; asst. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Mar.. 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; regis. gen., Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926; ag. gov., Fiji and high commnr., W. Pacific, Nov., 1927 to Apr., 1928 and from Apr., 1929.

SEYMOUR-HALL CHARLES CYRIL WILLIAM, F.R.S.A., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.C.I., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1919; capt., H.M.A.Z., 31, 1919; demob., 1919; asst. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. asst. admstr.-gen., 1926-27; ag. asst. sec., pvt. sec. to Br. res., clk. of couns. and edr., offl. ag., 1928; ag. prov. commnr. (with seat on leg. coun.), 1929.

SHANNON, C.M.—B. 1869; cler., asst., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1888; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1889; ditto, treas., June, 1890; 4th cls. clk., ditto, July, 1893; govt. offr. (mining regns.) and J.P., Apr., 1897; 3rd grade land survr., lands and mines dept., Aug., 1902; 3rd cls. clk., ditto, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1908; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1913; ch. clk.; P.W.D., July, 1920; supt., accts., corresp., stores, P.W.D., May, 1921; P.M.G., Feb., 1924.

SHARP, GEORGE FRIDERIC.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll. Schol. and King's Coll., London; compter and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on milly. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contg., 1914-18.

SHARPE, Sir ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897)—B. 1863; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyassaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. commnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy commnr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as commnr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; commnr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 16th July, 1897; reed. Jubilee medal, 1897; commnr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; reed. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyassaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

SHARPE, EDMUND.—B. 1882; ed. Arnold House, N. Wales, Haileybury and Edinburgh University; probationer, N.E. Rhodesia, Nov., 1905; asst. native commnr., Apr., 1906; native commnr., Dec., 1910; asst. mag., Apr., 1916; ag. mag. on various occasions, 1919 to 1927; mag., June, 1927; J.P.
SHARPE, H. B.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919; ag. dist. commnr., 1926.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prizeman in English prose; B.A., LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., 1900; collr., land rev., Krian, 1901; mag., 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr., Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr., land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr., Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supermny. offr., Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to W.O., directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land offr., Kedah, 19th July, 1919; ag. commnr., lands and mines, Johore, May, 1923; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; on sp. duty, Burma, Oct., 1925; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1925; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1927; gen. advr., Johors, Feb., 1929.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schol., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schol., Kingstown; asst. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, 25th May, 1921.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT, B.ACH. (1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll. Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908; to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admtd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisme justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stiltna, 23rd May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEDY, CAPT. FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., B.S., M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. Clongowes, Queen's Coll., Cork; late capt., R.A.P. forces; ent. army, A.V.C. 1911; vety. offr., R.A.P., July, 1914; served throughout Great War, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1919 (E. Africa Mounted Rifles and E.A.V.C.); adjutant, R.A.V.C., 9th Jan., 1916; M.B.E. (mil.), 1918, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med.; dep. sh. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; ag. ch. vety. offr., on various occasions 1920-21, 1923-24 and 1926; dep. dir., vety. services, Apr., 1927; ag. dir., vety. services, Jan. to Aug., 1929.

SHEEDY, ANDREW JOSPH.—B. 1879; joined S. Stiltna, pol., Jan., 1902; ag. 3rd cls. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1904; 3rd cls. inspr., Penang, Mar., 1906; 2nd cls. inspr., Penang, Aug., 1907; 1st cls. inspr., Penang, July, 1908; ag. ch. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1909; ag. ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, Apr., 1910; ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, July, 1910; ag. aast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; aast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Oct. and Dec., 1920; senr. aast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1923; dep. commnr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1925; ag. supt. and ch. pol. offr., Penang, Dec., 1925; ch. pol. offr., Langkawi, Sept., 1926; commnr., pol., Johore, Nov., 1927; ch. pol. offr., S'pore., Sept., 1928.

SHEFFIELD, JOHN NEWTON.—B. 1873; ed. Godolphin Sch., Lon.; civ. engng. wks. and surveys, 1892-96; survr., trig. survey, Perak, 1896; on rly. locn. and surveys, Perak, 1896-98; on trig. surveys, F.M.S., 1899-1908; asst. supdt., topo. surveys, F.M.S., 1909; dep. supdt., ditto, 1917; supdt., ditto, 1920; aast. survr.-gen., F.M.S., July, 1923.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1880; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. vice-consul, Ghent, 1906; ag. vice-consul, Havre, 1906-7; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; asst. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; sec. to first minister, 1909-10; collr. (now dist. commnr.), Peimsa, 16th Oct., 1912; ag. mag., 30th Aug., 1915; ag. ch. sec., 5th Mar. to 17th Nov., 1919; senr. commnr., Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1923; 4th cls. Order of the Aliyeh and 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar; M.L.C.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1902; ag. asst. supdt., immigrte., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to res.-gen., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1911; comm. in M.S. Vol. Rifles, 1911; ag. ast. regiar., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, 1914; ag. inspr.

SHELSWELL-WHITE, GEOFFREY HENRY.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schl.; served with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. comsnr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res. and 2nd asst. sec., secretariat, in 1921 to 1924; clk. of coun., 1924; ag. 1st asst. sec., 10th Sept. to 29th Dec., 1924.
SHELTON, C. F., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.D. (Lond.), B.S.—Civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judcl. olk., Nyaaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., E.A.P., 1913; ag. puisne judge, July, 1919; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Apr., 1920; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

SHERRINGHAM, GEOFFREY.—2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1899; resigned, 1908; war serv., Boer War, 1899-1902, France and G.E.A., 1916-18; K.A.R., 1918-19; asst. pol. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1919.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

SHIELS, DR T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch. B.); served European War as offr. R. Scots.; in command with rank as capt. one of trench mortar batteries of 9th (Scottish) Divn. (severely wounded); was a mem., Edinburgh town coun.; M.P., East Edinburgh since 1924; mem., sp. comm., Ceylon constitution, 1927; parly. under-sea., India Office, June, 1924; parly. under sec. for theCols., Dec., 1929.

SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. olk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911; regiar. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., promotions, 19th Nov., 1928.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, C.M.G. (1926), M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyassaland, 1908; transf. to Uganda, 1909; re-transf. as med. offr., Nyassaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.P., 1912; dep. prin. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; ag. P.M.O., Mar. Dec., 1924; P.M.O., Dec., 1924; dir., med. and sanry. services, 1926.

SHIRLEY, EVELYN PHILIP SEWALLIS, O.B.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columbus Coll., Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll., Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1928; 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade comsnr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1889; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1886) and Glasgow and Edin. Univs.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; civ. ser. comnr., Canada, 1908-1917; chmn. of historical manuscripts publication board, Oct., 1917; writer on history, banking and economics; del. to internat. congress of history of America, Rio de Janeiro, 1922; mem., preparatory comnr. for econ. confed., L. of N., Geneva, 1928.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permnt. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN DULPHUL, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripods, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comtce., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1929; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comtce., 1927; pvt. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 15th Sept., 1928; pvt. sec., permnt. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929.

SIDNEY, HON. THOMAS KAY, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1863; ed. prte. schls., Otago Boys' High Schl., and Otago Univ., N.Z.; barrister and solr., 1889; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1901-28; atty-gen. and leader of legis. coun., 1928.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; couzty inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until dishandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., 1st June to 14th Oct., 1925.

SIKES, HOWARD LECKY, B.A., B.E. (hons. and ext.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Bootham Schl., York; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engrnr. Mombasa water wks., 1911; engrnr. in ch., Magadi water wks. constr., 1913; res. engrnr., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneer, 1915; capt., 1916; govt. hydraulic engrnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.

SILVA, POTHDOWEE OSUNDU SAMUEL EDERSINGHE, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Puttalam kach., Mar., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Nov., 1928; office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Aug., 1929.
SILVESTER, J. M. — Ast. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1926.

SIMMONS, BENJAMIN ROBERT — Clk. in conservator of forest's office, E. conservancy, Transvaal, 1898; ag. dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1902; dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1906; ditto George, 1914; 2nd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1929.

SIMMONS, JAMES WILLIAM — B. 1877; ed. at Malvern and Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; in charge of treasury, Seremban, May, 1901; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, Aug., 1901; ag. sec. sany. bd., Seremban, Apr., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls. in addn., 1904; 1st ast. collr. of land rev., Seremban, Jan., 1905; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1907; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, May, 1909; comsrr. of cust., Johore, Dec., 1910; comsrr. of lands and mines, Johore, Oct., 1914 to June, 1920; sec. to comsrr. of enquiry, Trengganu, Sept., 1918; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1921; ast. to col. secretariat, Singapore, June, 1921; 1st ast. sec. A., S. Stiltns., June, 1921; ag. finan. comsrr., Johore, Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., S. Stiltns., May, 1922; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, July, 1923; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1924; ag. Br. advisor, Trengganu, May, 1925; Br. advr., Trengganu, Sept., 1926; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1928; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, June, 1928.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS. — B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; reeig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMMS, HENRY, M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913). — B. 1881; sany. engnr., Gold Coast, 1908; sany. engnr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1926.
SIMPSON, JOHN — Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1892; transf., post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1895; survr., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Aug., 1921.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH — B. 1878; sub.-inspr., Beauteuland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; ast. comsrr., 1918.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN — B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine coms. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.I., 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admstn., 29th Dec., 1921.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH. (1918). — B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; bar- rister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Coms., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBEEK, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc. — Clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1893; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf., dept. of agr., 1907; transf., dept. of int., 1911; gov't. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf., dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, 3rd Mar., 1925.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT Mc DONNELL, KT. BACH (1927). — Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Temp., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. govr., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr., June, 1904, as res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904, for two and a half months in 1905, and from June to July, 1906; regis.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. comsrr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, 8th Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.- gen., 1919-20; seconded, puisne judge, Jamaica, 1920; 1st puisne judge, 1921; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. rules, 1926.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple). — B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; attd., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office ast., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928.

SKENE, DONALD TRENCH, M.B., Ch.B. — B. 1883; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schol. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., grade III, May, 1912; med. offr., grade II, Dec., 1912; war service, 1915-19; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1919; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, May, 1921; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Feb.-Oct., 1924.

SKETE, CEOLI CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.). — B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hon. nat. sci. tripods pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schl. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; ast. dir., agr., Barbados, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1924 and 1925; ast. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir., sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D. — B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 16th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD — B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; ast. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. govr., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. comsrr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., Dec., 1922; dep. prov. comsrr., July, 1927; prov. comsrr., Sept., 1928.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM — B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub.-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apr., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb., 1920; clk. to comsrr., clk. of couns. and educnal. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. ast. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; senr. bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927.
SKINNER, HARVEY — B. 1871; ed. Hurstpierpoint and Ardingly Colls.; ast. assist. treasy., N. W. Rhodesia, June, 1907; ag. acctnt., N. Rhodesia, on various occasions, 1917-21; ast.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—Gloucestershire R.F.A., 3rd Apr., 1908 to 9th July, 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 22nd Aug., 1910 to 21st Aug., 1913; E.A.P. Police, 17th Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1st Apr., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1st May, 1920 to 28th Feb., 1922; qualified in senior captain's course, Schl. of Musketry, Hythe, Sept., 1921; supt. pol., Nyasaland, 11th Nov., 1922; ag. ch. comsnr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 17th May, 1923 to 31st Jan., 1924; asst. commdt., pol., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; ag. commdt., pol. and gov., prisons, Oct., 1925 to June, 1926.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNALL.—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsnr., income tax, May, 1927; prte. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929.

SKINNER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER GEORGE.—B. 1865; joined N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1879; offr.-in-charge, Lyttelton prison printing office, 1889; overseer, govt. printing office, 1906; supt., 1909; govt. printer, 1922.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helens, Nov., 1899 to Oct., 1902 (Queen's med. and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helens, Mar., 1903; served in various off. capacities in St. Helena, Antigus, Montserrat and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag. Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica Defence Force, 4th Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, 13th Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contingent B.W.I.R., Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-charge records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominics, 1st Nov., 1917 to 29th Oct., 1920; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in charge of prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depts., Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1923; re-transf. to Antigua as ch. keeper of prisons, supt., St. John's Training Schl. and supt., pauper cemetery, Leeward Is., Nov., 1924; dep. coroner, 1926.
SLACK, ERIC, M.B., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1872; med. offr., Basutoland leper asylum, 1919; war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-17.

SLACK, KENNETH HUGH.—B. 1885; ag. clk., mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, in 1905, 1906 and 1907; clk. and inspr. wks., P.W.D., Jan., 1909; rev. offr., Dieppe Bay, Apr., 1910; govt. offr. and harbrmr., Sandy Point, July, 1914; 3rd rev. offr., Apr., 1915; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1916; 2nd rev. offr., Jan., 1917; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1919; dep. harbrmr., Apr., 1920; ag. cashier, 1920; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1922; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force.

SLADE, WALTER, A.M. Inst. T.—B. 1877; L. and Y. rly., 1892; minor appts., S. Africa, S. America and India; E. African mily., rly., 1917; gen. foreman, Tanganyika rly., Apr., 1919; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., Mar., 1921.
SLADR-HAWKINS, W.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Ent. Jamaica Govt. serv., 1883; 1st cls. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. reg., 1895-7; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; capt., 1899; passed exam. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; ag. comsnr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; J.P. and stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910; res. mag., Clarendon, 1912; ditto, St. Ann, 1914; major, res. regt., 1914; res. mag., St. Elizabeth, 1922; ditto, St. Catherine, 1926; compiled 3rd edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1911; ed. 5th edit., "Sub-Officer's Guide," 1924.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyalawala, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1906; additional asst. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apr., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on special duty in Togoland, last to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. gov'r., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 4th Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griqua and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1890-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM.—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Krian, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1916; ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Johore, Sept., 1918; ditto Muar Johore, June, 1920; ag. asst. dir., educon., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1922; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1923; town-planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1926; ag. finan. comsnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IB, finan. comsnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, June, 1927.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-13; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong; med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly.; med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; ag. ast. M.O.H., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lei Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr., govt. civil hosp., 1923; med. offr., lunatic asylum, 1923; ag. port med. offr., 1924; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories; med. offr. in charge pub. mortuary, Kowloon and Kowloon pub. hosp., 1925; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1927; ag. dep. dir., med. and sany. services, 1928.
SMART, L. M.—B. 1889; ast. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 26th Mar., 1927.
SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PECT. Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; ast. audtr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., 1st Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1902; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comm., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. c.k., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., 19th May-31st Oct., 1924.

SMIT, JACOBUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekorative Trouwe Dienst) Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; pte. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; ast. mag., Transvaal, 1909; ast. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. for Klensdorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925; admtr., Transvaal, Mar., 1929.

SMITH, LIEUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-18; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harbmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, 27th Feb., 1926.
