SMITH, B. O.—Cler. astt., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions comm., 1920; sec., deptl. salaries comtee., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comsrs., July, 1923; transf. to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt., sup. ct., Oct., 1925.

SMITH, CHARLES BERNARD.—B. 1887; ed. Bedford House Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford (M.A.); supt. educn., N. Nigeria, 1913; addl. Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; senr. supt., 1919; ag. dir. educn., N. Provs., 1926; ast. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1927; ag. dep. dir., educn., Nigeria in 1928 and 1929.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. exped. force, 1917-18; barrister, solic., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z., 1920-27; author of "Adoption of Children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct., 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., Dec., 1913; on mil. serv., Deo., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; prizeman in consttit. law and hist.; ast. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 29th Aug., 1908; ast. pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., 29th Jan., 1914; transf. to legal dept., 10th Sept., 1914; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921, and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, 9th Aug., 1922; puisne judge, G. Coast, 12th June, 1925; compiled 1928 edition Laws of Gold Coast, Ashanti, etc.

SMITH, FREDERIC GORDON—B. 1886; ed. Broomsgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa with N. R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia during B.S.A. Co.'s adminstr. including regisr., high ct. etc., and ast. legal adviser and pub. pros.; ast. atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927.

SMITH, F. M. URLING.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs., 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C. M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; ast. to ch. sec., Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, Aug., 1886 to Feb., 1887; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1886 to Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., May to Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comsrr., Papho, Nov., 1891; regisr.-gen. and offi. mem. of legis. coun., June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comitee of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comitce., 1887; mem. of land registn. comsrr., 1891; pres. mun. comsrr., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. comsrr., 1895; mem. of mun. comsrr., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf, under conven. with Turkey, Mar., to Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; Br. del. of Evcaf, July, 1906; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1906, 1906, 1907 and 1908; col. sec., Mauritius, June, 1910; administered gov't., Apr., to Nov., 1911; gov', Nyasaland, May, 1913; assumed gov't., Sept., 1913; ret., Dec., 1923.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECHELIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. R. Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1896; hon. capt. in army, 1900; ast. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub.-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, 1902-04; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, 1904-06; ast. dist.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub-inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regisy. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; appld. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEE.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comanr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regis. of shipping) comanr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regis. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. acctn. comanr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Haatings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. ast. astt. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. ast. postmstr-gen., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, 1914; ag. postmstr-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. deputy regis. and appraiser, 1919; ag. supk. Victoria Gaol, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; qualified in Pekinese, Mar., 1920; supt., impts. and expts., 1921; atttd., Geneva opium confce., 1923; head of any. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1928.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munio. E. (Lond.)—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, archs. and survs., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as astt.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contra., Lond.; astt. to Mr. C. F. Wilke, M.I.C.E., city engnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.
SMITH, PETER HAMBLIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1900; ed. Denstone coll. and Lincoln coll., Oxford; astt. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1923.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON.—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, REGINALD MONTAGU BOWSWORTH, C.B.E. (1929).—Sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1896; inspr., 1906; astt. comanr., 1909; govt. sec., 1920; ag. dep. res. comanr. for various periods in 1921, 1923 and 1924.

SMITH, ROBERT, LL.B., Barrister and Solicitor.—B. 1886; ed. Pub. Schls., Nanaimo, B.C.; Osgoode Hall and Toronto Univ. (LL.B., 1912); called to bar, Ontario, 1912; called to bar, Br. Columbia, 1913; M.L.A., Vancouver, Br. Columbia.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, C.B.E. (1929), F.R. Aus. H.S.—B. 1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N. S. Wales, 13th June, 1912 to 29th Feb., 1920; astt. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, SYDNEY RICHARD, O.B.E. (Mil.), 1919.—B. 1875; ast. engr., P.W.D., Selangor, Apr., 1900; dist. engr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1901; ag. ex. engr., grade III, Sept., 1902; ag. ex. engr., grade II, May, 1904; ex. engr., grade III, Perak, Dec., 1909; ex. engr., grade II, Jan., 1913; adjt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1915; ment. in despatches, July, 1918; sen. ex. engr., P.W.D., Jan., 1919; ag. state engr., Selangor, May, 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., June, 1922; ag. state engr., Pahang, Jan., 1923; state engr., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1923; ag. ast. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; ag. state engr., Selangor, Apr., 1925; ag. state engr., Perak, Dec., 1926; state engr., Selangor, Dec, 1926; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1927; ag. ast. D.P.W., F.M.S., July, 1928; ag. dep. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1928.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; ast. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Stitlme., Jan., 1908; ast. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engr., grade III, July, 1908; ag. state engr., Kedah, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; sen. ex. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engr., Kedah, Apr., 1927 to May, 1928.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Bengal).—ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, 27th Nov., 1925.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb., Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripus, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; ast. audr., Nyasaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished comrn., owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., 29th Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, 29th Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITH-DORRIEN, GEN. SIR HORACE LOCKWOOD, G.C.B. (1913), G.C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1886); grand off., legion of honour; A.D.C. gen. to the King, 1910-14.—B. 1858; served in Zulu war, 1879; Egyptian war, 1882; Soudan campaign, 1885; Soudan field force, 1885-6, 4th cls., Medjidieh; 4th cls., Osmanieh, Chitral exped., 1890; N.W. Frontier, India, 1897-98; Tirah exped. force, Nile expedition, 1898; S. African war, 1900-01; European war, 1914-15; adjt.-gen. in India, 1901-3; commdg. Quetta div. of army in India, 1903-4; commdg.-in-chief Aldershot, 1907-12; commdg.-in-chief southern commd., 1912-14; commdg. 2nd army corps and 2nd British exped. force in France, 1914-15; commdg. E. African exped. force, 1916; gov., Gibraltar, 9th July, 1918; assumed govt., 7th Sept., 1918, ret., 1923.

SMITHERS, ROBERT FRANCIS.—B. 1875; clk., atty.-gen.'s office, Natal, 1902; secy., tender bd., 1907; senr. clk., dept. of just., 1910; mem., pub. serv. comrn. of enquiry, 1917; pub. serv. inspr., 1921; mem.-sec., pub. hosp. enquiry comrn., 1926.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebenb scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripus, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in cmd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comte of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confere. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.L.A. for Standerton, 1924.

SMYLV, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH., (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1896; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911.
SMYTHE, PAUL RODNEY.—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1928; attd. secretariat, Dec., 1928; attd., Putalam kach., July, 1929.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Kings Coll., London; clk., td. of educn., 1906; clk., C.A., 1910; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supt., 1917 and 1919; land ast., 1919; sec., land tenure comrn., 1920; warden of mines, 1920; registr. of titles, etc., 1922; ast. land offr., 1922.

SNELL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; ast. post office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; ast., G.P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., accs. branch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apr., 1912; income tax office, Pretoria, Sept., 1914; senr. clk., income tax office, Cape Town, July, 1915; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Mar., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; ch. clk., income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; ast. comrn. inland rev., Sept., 1929.

SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Copyist, med. dept., Jamaica, Oct., 1885; 3rd cls. clk., 1888; 2nd cls. clk., 1891; 1st cls. clk. and sec., cent. bd. of health, 1908; ch. clk., 1920; sec. to comrn. on reorganization of med. serv., 1920.

SOLOMON, NEVILLE STAFFORD VICTOR.—B. 1874; clk., G.P.O., Bahamas, 1891; tel. dept., 1892; treas. and cust., 1895; supervisor, cust., 2nd gde., Gold Coast, 1902; ag. ch. regir., sup. ct., 1906; finan. ast., treas. and cust. dept., Nigeria, 1907; supervisor, 1st gde., cust. dept., 1910; ocllr., 1917; admry. reporting offr., various ports, Nigeria, during great war, 1914-18; ret. on pension, 1922; ast. postmr. (temp.), Bahamas, Jan., 1924; resigned, Nov., 1925; rear. gen. and treas., and mem. exec. coun., Bahamas, 1928; has held various ag. appts.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1907), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town and Cambridge Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1876; raised to the bench, Kimberley, 1887; afterwards transferred to Eastern Districts Court in same capacity; puisne judge sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appellate div. of sup. ct. of South Africa, May, 1910; ch. just., 1st Mar., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo Kach., Dec., 1922; office astt. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office astt., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th astt. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th astt. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1929.
SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; govt. pilot, Kenya, 1924; astt. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., Aug., 1929.

SOMMERVILLE, JAMES O.—Clt., traffic dept., Cape, 20th Mar., 1897; clk., C.T. M. office, Cape, 1st Oct., 1898; ch. clk., admstr.'s office, Transvaal, 1st Dec., 1900; sec., refugees aid dept., 1st Nov., 1901; sec., Transvaal immigrn. office, 1st Aug., 1903; ch. clk., lands dept., 1st May, 1904; under sec. for lands, Union of Africa, 1st June, 1917; sec. for lands, 1st Apr., 1921.

SOBER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., Nov., 1926; agrl. offr., Jan., 1928.

SOUTHBOURGH, 1ST BARON (great. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1896), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; astt. lawalk., B. of T., 1885-1888; astt. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec. rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon off. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comtn., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comtn., 1906; mem. of comtn. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comtn. on electoral reform, 1909; registr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comtn., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHRN, WILFRID THOMAS, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1906; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr., customs, Colombo, July, 1909; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to govt., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. astt. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port commn., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1924 and exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., in 1927, 1928 and 1929.
SPARROW, WILLIAM LA BART.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; seconded to Weihaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928.

SPEARMAN, BARUCH, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Camb.), D.T.M. and H., certif., London Schl. of Trop. Medicine (with distinction).—B. 1877; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1912; capt., Uganda med. serv., Sept., 1916; med. offr. of blth., Zanzibar, Nov., 1920; recl. thanks of ag. gov., Uganda, for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Jinja, Nov., 1914; ment. in depts., 1919; M.O.H., Zanzibar, Sept., 1920; senr. med. offr. of health, Zanzibar, Jan., 1922; ag. D.M.S.S., on various occasions, 1922-23, 1925-26 and 1928; D.S.D.S., Jan., 1926.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripso, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.

SPENCE, ARNOTT VERE.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., treasry., St. Vincent, Nov., 1910; ag. rev. and excise offr., June, 1915 to May, 1916; 1st cls. rev. offr. and port offr., May, 1916; ag. warden, Leeward dist., June, 1917 to June, 1919; 2nd clk., govt. office, July, 1919; ag. clk. clk., govt. office, clk. ex. and leg. couns. and med. regis., Apr., 1923 to Sept., 1927; ch. clk. etc., Sept., 1927.

SPENOER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1893; also clk. to legis. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comnr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspir., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comdt., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in comd. of div. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of pris. and astt. to dist. comnr. and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comnr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf. to B. East Africa, as astt. dist. comnr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comnr., Mombasa, from July, 1907; dist. comnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1912; senr. astt. sec., secretariat, Jan., 1919; clk. of councils, Jan., 1919; ag. astt. chief sec., Jan., 1919; comnr. of prisons, Kenya, Oct., 1923.

SPICER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary astt. supl. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; astt. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I, 1923; comnr. pol., Kenya, June, 1925.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Sch. (Som.); served in engng. dept., G.W.R., 1896 to 1910; joined engng. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrg., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stilmt., 1920; ch. engrg. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., F.M.S. rly., and ch. engrg., 1926.

SPIO-GARBRABAH, JOSEPH.—B. 1878; 2nd ast. mast., tech. schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Jan., 1899; 1st ast. mast., 19th Nov., 1902; 3rd grade teacher, 1st Jan., 1907; prin. teacher, 3rd Nov., 1912; ast. headmast., 1st Apr., 1920; headmast., 1st Apr., 1922; insp. schls., 1st Apr., 1923.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. ast. engrg., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; ast. engrg., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engrg., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engrg., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engrg., grade I, Pohp., Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engrg., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; ag. engrg. constrn., F.M.S. rly., Sept., 1925 to Feb., 1927; senr. dist. engrg., Johore Bahru, Mar., 1927; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1928.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regis., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. regis., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. ast. regis. sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1905; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908; ag. regis., supreme ct., Penang, Dec., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1909; solr.-gen., Apr., 1911; ag. jud. comsnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1912; solr.-gen., May and Oct., 1912; ag. temp. judge, sup. ct., July-Sept., 1912, and Nov., 1912; puisne judge, Dec., 1913; pres., toddy comsnr., Selangor, Oct., 1916; ag. ch. jud. comsnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., S. Stilmt., Oct., 1920; ag. sen. puisne judge, Feb.-Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just., S.S., 16th June, 1922; ag. ch. jud. comsnr., F.M.S., June, 1924.

STACE, WALTER TERENCE.—B.A., Trin. coll., Dublin; B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 29th Oct., 1910; attached to the Galle Kachcheri, 1st Dec., 1910; office astt. to govt. agt., Galle, March, 1912; pol. mag., Chilaw, May, 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; priv. sec. to gov. and extra ast. col. sec., Sept., 1915; ast. censor, Dec., 1916; censor, May, 1918; dist. judge, Negombo, Apr., 1920; ast. astlmt. offr., Jan., 1922; ag. astlmt. offr., Mar.-Nov., 1926; mayor of Colombo, Oct., 1927 to June, 1928.
STALLWORTHY, HON. ARTHUR JOHN.—B. 1877; ed. State schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. parl., 1928; min. of health, 1928.

STANFORD, COL. THE HON. SIR WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, K.B.E. (1919), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1892), O.B.E. (1898).—B. 1850; mag.'s clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gaoleka war, 1877-8 (medal and clasp); mem. native laws and customs comsnr., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1 (med. and clasp); on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1886; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; comd. forces in E. Griqualand, Boer War, 1899-1902 (depts., 2 meds. and 2 clasps); ditto and ch. mag., July; 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs comsnr., Sept., 1903, to Jan. 1906; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; mem. of senate, Union of S. Africa, 1910-29; dir., war recruiting, Union of S. Africa and commissioner for returned soldiers, 1918-19.
STANLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR LULPH, K.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1875; ed. Oxford Univ. (B.A.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; M.P. for Eddisbury div. of Cheshire, 1906-10; gov. of Victoria, 1914-20.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; ast. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov.-gen., 1913; resident comsnr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comsnr., for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1927.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.K. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjkt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expedit., 1901, (med. and clasp); ag. trav. comsnr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901: A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. comsnr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. comsnr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, AMBROSE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1929), M.D. (Toronto), F.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Cambridge).—B. 1875; senr. house surg., regis. and demonstr., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., 1905-07; bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1908; on sp. duty in Siam, Indo-China and Netherlands E. Indies, 1915-16; dir., govt. labs., F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser to S. of S., 1926; chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comte., 1926; chmn., managing bureau hygiene and trop. disease, 1926; mem. col. med. research comte., 1927; Craggs' research prize, L.S.T.M., 1911; Bisset Hawkins gold med., Royal Coll. of Physicians, London, 1926; Mary Kingsley Memorial Med., Liverp. S.T.M., 1929; author of "Etiology of Beriberi," "Malayan Culicidae," "Meloidoidea," etc.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; appld. after compet. exam., ast. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922.

STARK, WALTER JOHN KINKEPATRICK.—B. 1887; ed. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and at Aberdeen Univ. (M.A.), 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., Klang, Mar., 1911; ditto, Ulu Langat, May, 1911; ditto, Klang, May, 1912; passed cadet, Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imigrts., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr., lab., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1913; asst. contr., lab., Seremban, Apr., 1914; ag. mag., Seremban, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1915; asst. contr., lab., Klang, Sept., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. dep. contr., lab., Sept., 1917 and June, 1918; dep. contr., lab., Klang (offr., cls. IV.), 1st Jan., 1919; emigr. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Coconada, Sept., 1920; ag. emigrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Madras, 9th to 24th Oct., 1920; supt., emigrn. depot, Negapatanam, 31st Mar., 1922; mag., Ipoh, 28th July, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Dec., 1926; ditto, Klang, Oct., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Nov., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1928; mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1928.

STARNES, COBTLANDT.—B. 1864; served in N.W. Rebellion, 1885, as ag. adjutant, 65th Montreal; Inspr., R.N.W.M.I. Pol., 1st Mar., 1886; adjutant, 1891-97; supt., 1909; asst. commr., 1st Dec., 1919; commr., 1923.

STATEN, OSCAR WILLIAM—Aast. res. mag., Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1902; ag. res. mag. and res. mag., Barberton, 1902-11; res. mag. Heidelberg, 1911-16; insprg. mag., 1916-21; ch. pub. serv. inspr., 1921; sec., pub. wks., Jan., 1925.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. parlt. since 1915; speaker since 1923.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILSONS, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbour and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; reglar. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, 1st Jan., 1919 to 31st Mar., 1920; refugee commr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; reglar. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. commr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; reglar. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; reglar. of companies, July, 1922; reglar. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; reglar. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. reglar.-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; reglar. trade marks and patents, 1st Apr., 1928; ag. reglar. companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of imigrn. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.

STEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY, O.B.E. (1929), M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll. and trained at Royal Naval Depot, Portland; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Proct., 1905; ag. exec. engrnr., Kisumu, Mombasa, 1907; in charge, Thika rly. survey and constrn., 1910-11; ag. exec. engrnr., Nairobi and Naivasha, 1912-16; seconded to 3rd batt., K.A.R., 1915 ("1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meda.); exec. engrnr., P.W.D., 1917; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1925; Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, cls. III., March, 1928.
STERED, L.F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.
STEEL, GEOFFRICHARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.) L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H.T.M. (distincte) D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Milly. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 26th Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, 7th July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, 15th Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and reglar.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920.

STEEL-MAITLAND, SIR A. H. D. R. BART. (great. 1917).—B. 1876; ed. Rugby (scholar and exhibitioner); Belliol Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); Eldon scholar, 1899; 1st cls., class. mods., 1897; 1st cls. class. schls., 1899; 1st cls. final law schls., 1900; sec., junr. treas. and pres., Oxford Univ. Soce.; special commr. to royal commr. on Poor Laws, 1906-7; M.P. (C.) East Birmingham, since 1910; chrmn. of Unionist party organisation, 1911; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 31st May, 1915; ditto, 11th Dec., 1916; additional under-sec., F.O., and additional parly.-sec., B. of T., in charge of the work of the dept. of overseas trade, 1917-1919; min. of lab., 7th Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; commd., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in desps.; ch. commr., pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1869; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1894; inspr., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, 4th Jan., 1912, and mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, 2nd Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, 30th Aug., 1915 to 20th Apr., 1916 and from 26th Feb. to 5th Aug., 1918; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Feb., 1925; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Naval Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edtn.); asst. editor of 11th edtn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter parties" etc.; contributor to the Encyclopaedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENSON, LIEUT.-COL. ARTHUR, C.M.G., (1918), D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1881; served S.A. War (Queen's med., 3 clasps); S. Rhodesia civ. serv., 1902-04; N.R. civ. serv., 1904-12; served in Great War, 1915-19; (deeps.); lieut.-col., Reg. Army R. of O. (R. Scots), 1916; commdt., N.R. Volunteer Forces, Aug., 1919; ch. intell. offr., N.R., June, 1920; ag. commdt., N.R.P., Oct., 1924; commdt., April, 1925; J.P.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD.—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914, to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; appd. under re-construction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923.

STERLING, HERBERT HARRY, LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. Christchurch (N.Z.) Boys High Sch., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. rly. dept., 1901; mem., rly. bd., 1924; ret., 1926; gen. man., rlys., 1928.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Sch., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hons., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, WILLIAM JOHN COTTON.—B. 1879; ed. Grey Inst., Port Elizabeth, St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Univ. of Cape of Good Hope; survr., grade I, F.M.S., June, 1909; ag. asst. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., Aug., 1917; ag. asst. supt., survs., Kedah, Aug., 1918; asst. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., 1919; ag. supt., survs., Kedah, Aug., 1920 and Feb., 1923; supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1925; supt., survs., Kedah, July to Aug., 1925; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Mar., 1926.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; att., Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Aruwewella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kegalle, Sept., 1928.

STEVENSON, CECIL YOUNG, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., F.B.Met.Soc.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; capt., R.E., 1915; adjt., 1916; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Exp. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mons Star," ment. in despatches; exec. engrnr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. D.P.W., Apl. to Dec., 1920; ag. dep. D.P.W., Jan., 1921, Nov. to July, 1924 and June, 1929.

STEVENSON, DUNCAN.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Schl., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1918; lieut., R.E., 1919; France, 1918-19; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conservator, forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conservator, forests, Apr. to Oct., 1927; senr. asst. conservator, forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924.

STEWARD, THOMAS ALFRED FRANK.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African war with Army Ser. Corps.; ch. clk. in staff office for payment of mil. receipts, O.R.C., 1902-1903; registr. of war claims, Kronstad and Heilbronn districts, O.R.C., 1903-1904; clk., Swaziland adminstr., July, 1906; principal clk., Apl., 1910; also registr. and master of the special ct. of Swaziland and sheriff of Swaziland, Apl., 1907; passed Cape Univ. civ. ser. lower law exam., 1906; ag. asst. comrnr., Peak dist., 1916-18; asst. comrnr., 1919.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922; dir., govt. cottage hosps., Natal, 1922.

STEWART, CHARLES A.—Elec. to Alberta legis., 1909-21; min. of munici. affrs., 1913; min. of pub. wks., 1913; premier of Alberta and min. of rlys. and telephones, 1917; ag. min. of immigrn. and colonization, 1921-23; min. of int. and mines and supt.-gen. of Indian affrs. in Dominion govt., 1921 and 1926.

STEWART, DONALD JAMES.—B. 1876; ed. privately, and at Whitgift Schl.; mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; senr. clk., 1903; ag. dist. registrar, 1906; prin. clk., 1908; ag. asst. registrar, 1910; asst. registrar, 1921; regtar. and Rand townships registrar, 1923; served in S. African War, 1899-1901, and the Great War.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apl., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and agt. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and govt.'s commr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1916; ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to admistr., June, 1912; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examiners, Fijian language, Dec., 1913; asst. native commr. and govt.'s commr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; asst. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1918; liut., Fiji defence force, July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; ag. dist. commr., Rewa and prov. commr., Rewa and Naitasari, 1918; asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; priv. asst. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; agt. gen. immigrn. and editor "Radjut" in 1922 and 1923; ag. col. sec., etc., 2nd Sept., 1922 and 18th Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov.'s dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. commr., W. Pacific H.C. and agt. asst. high commr. and gov't. dep., Sept., Oct., 1925; gov't. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns. and exec. comtees. also ch. bd. of dirs., sugar industry agrl. bank; ag. gov., Apr.-July, 1927 and Feb., 1929; rep. col. at 1st standing W. Indian Conf., 1929.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory medals; ment. in despatches; vety. off., Gold Coast, 27th Dec., 1923.
STEWART, NIL, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; supt., 1927.
STEWART, HON. PERCY GERALD.—B. 1885; el. mem., H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Wimmera, Dec., 1919; min. of pub. works and rlys. C. of A., 1923-24.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Sudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmth; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROBT. PETER.—Exhbtr., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1929-33 and 1939-43; clk., immigrn. dept., 1894; seconded to govt. secretariat, 1897-98, and 1900-02; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, July, 1903; immigrn. agt., Dec., 1906; ag. sen. immigrn. agt., 1908, 1909, and 1913; senr. immigrtn. act., Feb., 1914; mem., bd. of examtns. in Hindi, etc., 1914; a rep. of Br. Guiana at educn. conf., Trinidad, 1921; chmn., poor law comms., 1920-22; dir., widows and orphans pension fund; mem., bd. of educn., colonisation and development bd., lab. exchange bureau and various comtes.; ag. comsnr., educn., Sept., 1926 to June, 1927; ag. immigrtn. agt.-gen., on various occasions, 1916-26; mem., exec. coun. in 1924, 1925 and 1926.

STEWART, ROBERT STROTHER, M.A., B.C.L., B.Litt., Durham Univ., Gladstone prizeman.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynemouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr. commdg. Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1915-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser, C.O., July to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, Jan., 1930.
STEYN, N. J. R.—B. 1876; prin. O.N.O. schl., Lastron, O.F.S., 1904-5; vice-prin., 1906-20; prin., 1920-22; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1st Apr., 1922.

STIEBEL, HERBERT CECIL, O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1876; Boer War, 1899-1902 (King's and Queen's meda.); Transvaal civ. serv., as sub-native comsnr., 1902; prot. of natives, 1907-16; South African rebellion, 1914; E. Africa campaign, 1916; polit. dept., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; senr. comsnr., Apr., 1920; prov. comsnr., Apr., 1926.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON, I.S.O. (1929).—Ed. Rugby; 2nd clk. to res. comsnr. for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and registr. to res. comsnr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899 to May, 1900; clk. of ct. and clk. to asst. comsnr., Gaberones, 1902; J.P., Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Feb., 1904; Dec., 1906 to Feb., 1906, and May, 1906 to Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag. for Southern dist., Bechuanaland Prot., Jan., 1907; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; res. mag., N'gamiland, 1910; hon. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914; awarded "1914-15" star for services as mily. intell. offr., German S.W. Africa Campaign; lieut. R.W. Kent Regt., 1916; served B.E.F., France; attd., Intell. I.E.F., Italy, Oct., 1917; ment. in desps.; temp. capt., Jan., 1919; mil. control offr., Italy, to Nov., 1919; capt., R.W. Kent Regt. R. of O., Mar., 1923; awarded Murchison grant by R.G.S., May, 1923 for surveys and map of N'gamiland; res. mag., Kweneng dist., Jan., 1924.
STIRLING, Sir JOHN LANGLELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), O.B.E. (1918), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres. legis. coun., since 1901.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, C.B.E. (1925) M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibn., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.), 1904; M.A., 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; sci. sec. to W.I. agric. conf., 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. comen., 1909; mem., banana coman., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comte., 1911; comsnr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regiar., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irrigation comte., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regist., co-op. socie- ties and vice-pres., agril. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comte., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to Imp. entomological confce., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to Imp. botanical confce., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management, tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dists., N. India with Ceylon delegation, 1925; asst. agril. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several repts., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON.—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; priv. secr. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

STONE, ARNOLD ALFRED PRICE DUNBAR, O.B.E. (1929), F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy., E., A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1877; eng. draughtsman, Sierra Leone, 1912; ag. asst. D.P.W., Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. col. eng., Gambia, Jan., 1920; dist. eng., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., May to Oct., 1922 and May to Oct., 1924; asst. D.P.W., 1923; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1925; mem., leg. coun., 1925; ag. gen. man. rly. in July., 1927.

STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African Rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1911; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1914; ag. senr. comsnr., 1927 and 1928; senr. comsnr., 2nd grade, 1929.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (or. 1925), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C. (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; prte. sec. to Sir U. Garatin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, desps., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Avr Burghs, 1922-26; parly. mem. Air Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transport, 1922-24; 1st comsnr. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 10th Aug., 1925.

STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN.—B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; asst., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; survr.'s staff, N.dist., Dec., 1901; engrn. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmtr., Feb., 1904; postmtr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmtr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; survr., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. survr., 1916; survr., survr., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G., various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 8th Oct., 1920; mem. legis. coun., July, 1926.
STORRS-FOX, D., LL.D.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

STORRS, Sir Ronald, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.T. Bach. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919). —B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripes, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo- French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (deeps.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comte, Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917- 20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc., Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local gov't. com., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; apptd. gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 16th Sept., 1926; assumed duty, 30th Nov.,

STOUT, The Rt. Hon. Sir Robert, P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. sol., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educ., 1884-87; fellow and ex-chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1899-1926; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.; M.L.C., 1926.

STOUTE, Cyril Eustace.—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., 9th Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., 14th July to 24th Aug., 1926 and 20th Apr. to 14th July, 1927.

STOYLE, Herbert Blin.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Battersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. comntr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923.

STRACHAN, John, M.I.C.E., M.I.St.E.—B. 1877; ed. at Bath; ch. engr., lake development scheme, Colombo, Ceylon, Aug., 1911; miny. of transport, 1919; asst. D.P.W., Ceylon, 1920; asst. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., in 1921 and 1923; D.P.W., F.M.S., chmn., elec. bd. and mem. rly. bd., Apr., 1923; D.P.W., Ceylon and mem., leg. coun., chmn., elec. bd., pub. wks. advisory bd., local gov't. bd. and prison bd., pres. engng. asan., 1923-26; gen. man. and ch. engr., F.M.S. rly., chmn., rly.; bd., Jan., 1927; offr. mem. fed. coun., F.M.S., Oct., 1927.
STRACHAN, Peter Donald, M.D.—B. 1872; med. offr., Bechuana Land Prot., 1921-23; supt., Basutoland leper asylum, 1923.

STRACHEY, Sir Charles, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comttee, 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 26th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtce, 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1913; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtees, for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.

STRADBROKE, (3rd Earl of) (or. 1821). George Edward John Mowbray Rous, Baron Rous (or. 1796), Viscount Dunwich (or. 1821), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888- 1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria,

STRAHAN, Kenneth Cyriar.—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and King's Coll., Cambridge; Caylon rly., 1908-16; R.A.S.C.M.T., 1915; capt., G.H.Q., Inspection Branch, 1917; ment. in depts.; ch. mech. engr., Tanganyika rly., 1920.
STRAKER, Major G.H., M.C., B.A. (Oxon). —Asst. dist. comntr., Kenya, Oct., 1921.

STRATFORD, James.—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, England, 1898; Transvaal, 1902; practised Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. division, 1st March, 1921; judge of appeal,

STRATHAIRN, Donald Beith.—B. 1884; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; passed law agent's exam.; asst. dist. comntr., Gold Coast, 1912; dist. comntr., 1918; senr. asst. col. sec., 1921; clk. of couns., 1922; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., for various periods, 1922-25; ag. sec. for native affairs.,

STRATHAIRN, George Cecil, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.). D.P.H., certif. from London S.T.M.— Med. offr., Orange River Colony Burgher Camps, 1901-03; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1903; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1914; war serv. with rank of major, 1914-18; ch. med. offr., Fiji, 1920; mem., leg. couns., Fiji, 1920-22; senr. sanit. med. offr., Jamaica, 1922.
STREET, Sir Philip Whistleb, K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ch. just., 1925.

STRICKLAND, 1st Baron (or. 1928), Sir Gerald Strickland, Count della Catena, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of gov't., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripes; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar. Inn Temp., and pres. cholera comntr., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. assist. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health pres., comtce. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comte. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrns.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed gov't., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrns., N.S. Wales; held dormant coman. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1928.
STRINGER, HON. SIR THOMAS WALTER, K.T. BACH. (1929).—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONG, ARTHUR NESBITT.—B. 1890; M.A., Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 13th Dec., 1913; office ast. to gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Jan., 1914; ag. office ast. to gov't. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1914; addtl. coman. of requests, Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; on military duty, 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1919; landing surv., Colombo cust., Nov., 1920; dep. food contr. in addn., July, 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1924; ast. gov't. agt., Matare, Apr., 1925; temp. addl., C.O., Nov., 1927 to Apr., 1928; dep. collr., cust., May, 1928.
STRONG, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub. inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; inspr., 1929.

STRONG, THEOPHILUS BENJAMIN, M.A., B.Sc. (N.Z. Univ.)—B. 1871; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl., Canterbury Coll. and Univ. of Otago; teacher in primary, secondary and tech. schls., N.Z., 1887-1904; inspr., schls., 1904-15; senr. inspr., 1915-19; ch. inspr., primary schls., 1920-26; ast. dir., educn., 1926; dir., educn., 1927.

STRONGE, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.T. BACH. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—B. 1875; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., July to Aug., 1914, Sept. to Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; reed. thanks of Tongan govt. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of coman., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mil'y. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19; deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hemdy Rise, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUART, WALTER LESLIE.—3rd associate to judges, sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, S. Australia, 1898; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1900; 2nd associate and clk. of arraigns, Nov., 1905; judges' assoc., chief clk. of sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, Feb., 1906; also a solr. to lands titles office, 1911; master of sup. ct., regis. of probates, regis. in admiralty, regis. of companies, trades unions and industrial and provident societies, and dist. regis. of high ct. of Australia, Jan., 1913, after acting in these offices from 1911.

STUARTSON, COLLARD METHVEN.—Ck., cust., Fiji, Nov., 1912; clk., res. commr.'s dept., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Coly., Nov., 1913; dep. commr., W. Pacific, Oct., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Dec., 1918; dist. offr., July, 1926.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD CARLETON.—B. 1881; ent. R.N., 1886; lieut. (navigating offr.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron navigating offr., First Battle Squadron, 1920-23; ast., King's harbour, Portsmouth, 1923-26; in command, H.M.S. "Agamemnon" 1926-27; ret. with rank of capt., 9th Mar., 1927; master attendant, Colombo, 12th Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitor); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cl. clk., May, 1907; 1st cl. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Sultans, F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comte., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; offr. administering the govt., Jan. to Oct., 1913; Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov't., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed govt., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. LL.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt. gen. and gov. in ch., Jamaica, 1926; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT.—A.I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas., 1923.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, K.T. BACH. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr., Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-86; sup't and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comte., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20.
STURDY DONALD, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; agrl. course, 1923-26; B.A., 1926; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926.
STURGEES, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1871; M.R.C.V.S.; gov't. vet. surgeon, Ceylon, Mar., 1895.

STURROCK, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902 (hons. clas.); cadet, F.M.S., 1902; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, May, 1903; passed cadet, Oct., 1904; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Nov., 1907; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng, Mar., 1910; seconded for service in Kelantan, Apr., 1910; judge, high ct., and ag. supt. of cust., May, 1910; dist. offr., Batu Mengkabang, Dec., 1910; ditto, Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1913; supernmy. mag., Ipoh, Mar., 1915; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1915; ast. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Oct., 1915; supernmy. offr., cis. II., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1921; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, July, 1922 and Dec., 1924; ag. Fed. examr. in Malay, Apr., 1922; chmn., rubber restric. comte., Perak South in addn., Nov., 1922; offg. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1924; fedl. examr. in Malay, Nov., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. 1B, chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1927; ag. sec. agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. lir. advr., Trengganu, Sept., 1928; Br. adviser, Trengganu, June, 1928.

STURROCK, GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edin.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1907; ex. engrnr., grade III, Jan., 1913; ex. engrnr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1914; ditto, Jelebu, in addn., 1916; ditto, Klang, Aug., 1920; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1923; state engrnr., Kedah, Oct., 1926; dep. col. engrnr., S'pore, June, 1927; ag. col. engrnr., Straits Settlements, Dec., 1927; col. engrnr., S.S., 1929.

STURROCK, JOHN CHRISTIAN RAMSAY, C.M.G. (1927), M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; tutor to H.H. the Kabaka of Buganda, Mar., 1905; dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1914; prov. comsnr., June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., June, 1924; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1926.

STUTCHBURY, SAMUEL.—B. 1882; supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1909, and served in different depts. of the P.O.; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Singapore, Feb., 1913; supt. of mails, P.O., Penang, Jan., 1916; senr. supt. of mails, Juiy, 1918; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Jan., 1918; supt., grade I, Jan., 1919; ag. accont., G.P.O., May, 1919; supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, but contd. to act as accont., Singapore, Jan., 1920; ag. ast. P.M.G., May, 1920; accont., G.P.O., but contd. to act as ast. P.M.G., Sept., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1921; ag. ast. P.M.G., July, 1921; resumed duties as accont., Singapore, Nov., 1921.

SUARES, ALFRED RADIMOND.—B. 1874; 3rd cls. clk., regsr. gen.'s dept., Jamaica, 1891; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; supervisor, census, 1911; ast. regsr. gen. and ast. dep. keeper, records, 1913; supervisor, census, 1921; regsr. gen. and dep. keeper of records, 1922.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd. Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office ast. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; ag. archl. comsnr., Jan., 1926; office ast. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Nov., 1928; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matale, May, 1929.

SUFFERN, T. H., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), Cert. of London, S.T.M.—Apptd. to W.A.M.S., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; served in Cameroons E.F., Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1915; ment. in desps., May, 1916; tempy. capt. (general list), Oct., 1914 to July, 1915; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1920.

SULLIVAN, CHARLES ERIC EUGENE, B.A. (Oxford).—Ast. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 7th Dee., 1909; temp. capt., intell. dept., K. African Forces, 1916; ag. 1st ast. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1918; ag. ast. chief sec., July, 1918; ag. regar. gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Sep., 1918; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1919; senr. ast. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1925; ag. prov. comsnr., 1926; ag. deputy chief sec., Aug., 1926; ag. prov. comsnr., 1926; ag. dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1926; dep. prov. comsnr., 1926; prov. comsnr., 1st grade, 1928.
SULLIVAN-TAILLOUR, EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1886; supt., pol., Kenya, June, 1923.

SUMMERS, WAITE LLOYD.—B. 1870; entd. crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clk., agri. off., May, 1892; chief clk., July, 1902; ag. sec. to min. of agri., Feb., 1910; sec. to ditto, July, 1911; also sec. to min. of industry and sec. to min. of marine, 1915.
SUMNER, JAMES A. W.—B. 1903; ent. L.P.S. (Inland Rev.), May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transf., C.O., Oct., 1923.
SUMNER, WILSON GEORGE HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Chigwell Schol.; Colonial Fellow of Surveyors' Inst., 1916; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

SURIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; ast. ch. sec. office, Cyprus, 16th Mar., 1918; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1918; ag. ch. ast. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local comdt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June, 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. ast. sec., Oct., 1920; passed exam. in Turkish, Aug., 1922; ag. comsnr., Aug., 1922; ag. ast. sec., Sept., 1922; ag. comsnr., Dec., 1923 to Oct., 1923; comsnr., Jan., 1924.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoo., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr. law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice repts., Queb., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, A. R., M.A., Aberdeen University.—Science mast., Glen Urquhart H. G. schl., 1903-5; ast. mast., Campbeltown Gram. schl., 1906-6; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-10; ag. ast. mast., pol. schl., 1909 and 1912; headmast., Victoria British schl., 1913; lieut., R.G.A., on active serv., 1914-19; ast. mast., cls. I, Jan., 1920; headmast., Indian schl., 1920; ag. inspr., English schls., Feb. to Nov., 1925 and Mar., 1928; J.P., 1928.
SUTHERLAND, CHARLES MOHARDY HUNTER, B.Sc., B.Sc. (agr.).—Ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schol.; military serv., 1914-19; B.Sc., 1923; B.Sc. (agr.), 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.
SUTHERLAND-GREAME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. Rly., 1916-24; loco. supt., F.M.S. Rly., 1924.

SUTTON, GEORGE GERHARD, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Dale Coll., King William's Town, Union of S. Africa, Manchester Grammar Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1903; admitted to bar, sup. ct., Cape Town, Oct., 1903; K.C. for Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1921; ag. judge, Cape prov. divn., on various occasions, 1922-28; judge, Cape prov. divn., Feb., 1929.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), O.B. (mill.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1906).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1882; entd. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1901; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig.-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expdn., 1886-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaissance; in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing Pho country, Nov., 1896, to Moh., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay gvt., and appreciation by gvt. of India); Gildessa affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay gvt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E. Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in despe., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by gvt.); organized 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expdn., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in despe.); apptd. comsnr., consul-gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italia deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 1906-13; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by gvt. of India) 1908; spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon; exhibitioner, Worce. Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2nd class classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admstd. the gvt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admstd. gvt., Apr. to Nov., 1895; again in 1897; 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed gvt., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct., 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906; reed. coronation medal, 1911; gov., Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 11th March, 1912; assumed gvt., 26th July, 1912; ret., 1918; rep. of Fiji at Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Stltms., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.F. and mag. and comsnr., ot. of requesta, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. comsnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; comsnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. comsnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, Jan., 1890; admstd. gvt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comsn. on Mauritius, 1909.

SWETTENHAM, SIR J. ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll., Camb.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service, 1868; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. mag., Kayla, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. asst. gvt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; ag. landing and tide surv., Galle, June, 1872; asst collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to continue to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1873; 2nd asst. col. sec., and clk. of the legis. coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audr.-gen., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admstd. gvt., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1898, and from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov., Br. Guiana, 1901; gov., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; resign., 1907.

SWETTENHAM, LIMUT.-COL. JAMES PARRY, O.B.E. (1928), M.S.V.R.—B. 1875; ed. Shrewsbury and Bedford Grammar Schls.; Boer War, 49th Imperial Yeomanry (Montgomeryshire) 1899-1900 (Queen's med. with 3 bars); ast. engrnr., Gold Coast (Kumasi) Rly., 1901; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, F.M.S., 1902; exec. engrnr., grade II, Pahang, F.M.S., 1907; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 1911; capt., M.S.V.R., 1912; hon. A.D.C. to gov., S.S. and high comsnr., F.M.S., 1912-19; exec. engrnr., grade I, F.M.S., 1914; seconded for mily. serv. in the Great War; ast. to chf. engrnr., 2nd Army Oent. Force; temp. capt., R.E., 1915; O.C., 201st Field Co., R.E.; temp. major, R.E.; invalided from R.E., 1916; "1914-15 Star," War and Victory med.; commdt., M.S.V.R. 1919; state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, 1919; ast. dir. of pub. wks., F.M.S., 1923; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., 1923; ag. gen. man., rlys., F.M.S., Feb., 1926; on sp. duty, pub. wks. dep., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; M.I.C.E., 1927; Col., Aux. Forces Officers' Decorn. and Long Serv. Med., 1927; ast. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W., Dec., 1927; chmn., Fraser's Hill development comtee.; chmn., Cameron's Highland development comtee.; chmn., communications bd., F.M.S., all in addn., Dec., 1927; mem. rivers comsn., F.M.S., Mar., 1928; chmn., rivers advisory bd., F.M.S., Apr., 1928; D.P.W., F.M.S., July, 1928.

SWETTENHAM, ROGER FRANCIS ROSS.—B. 1883; ed. privately, Bedford and Aston Rowant; stltm. offr., Negri Sembilan, June, 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Coast, Negri Sembilan, July, 1908; ag. chmn. and sec., san. bd., Seremban, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1909; ag. sec., san. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Apr., 1910; aspt. chandu monopoly, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1910; supervisor of cust., Kuala Lumpur in addn., Apr., 1911; seconded for mily. serv. (R.G.A. Cadet Schl.) Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1918; aspt., chandu monopoly, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; sec., standing advisory comtee. on use of opium in Malaya, June, 1924; dep. chmn. and sec., ditto, Nov., 1924; dep. comsnr., trade and cust. (chandu), F.M.S., May, 1925; ag. comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., in 1926; ag. dep. comnr., trade and cust. (excise) in addn., Oct.-Dec., 1927; ag. comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., in addn., Apr. to May, 1928.

SWIFT, JOSEPH ARTHUR, B.Sc., B.E., M.I.E.S.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Tasmania; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1904; ag. ex. engnr., grade II, ast. to D.P.W., F.M.S., June, 1911; ex. engnr., grade III, Selangor, Apr., 1913; ex. engnr., grade II, F.M.S., June, 1914; sen. ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ag. state engnr., Selangor, Apr., 1923; ag. state engnr., Pahang, Apr., 1924; ag. state engnr., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; state engnr., Negri Sembilan, Dec., 1926.

SWINBOURNE, MAJOR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, O.B.E. (1913).—B. 1884; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt. rly., May, 1900; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; passed with honours in topography and range-finding, Sydney, 1908; clk., pay and qrtmr., with rank of sub-instr., Fiji constab., 6th Oct., 1909; capt., Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; commg. offr., Fiji cadet corps, 13th Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, 13th Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., 16th Apr., 1911; qualified rifle course, Hythe, May, 1914; qualified (distinguished) machine gun course, Hythe, June, 1914; attached 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fiji lat. contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., 1st Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, 13th Sept., 1915; commdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, 30th Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., 6th Oct., 1916; mentioned by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active service, July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., 30th Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., 25th Jan., 1918.

SWINDLE, REV. FRANK GUTHRIE, M.A. (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; col. chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. chap., Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908; ag. col. chap., Penang, 20th Dec., 1912; col. chap., Singapore, 1st Nov., 1914; regsr., diocese of Singapore, 13th Feb., 1915; archdeacon of Singapore, 2nd Feb., 1916; commissary, diocese of Singapore, 29th Sept., 1918; resigned regsr. of diocese, 3rd Feb., 1920; dep. regsr., July, 1921.

SYDENHAM OF COMBE, LORD (1st BARON, 1913, U.K.).—SIR GEORGE SYDENHAM CLARKE, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1905), G.C.S.I. (1911), G.C.I.E. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expdn., 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defense comtees, 1885-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comtees to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comtees, 1904; gov. of Bombay, 1907-1913.

SYDNEY, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. JOHN CHARLES WRIGHT, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1885-1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1889-1893; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1896; St. George's, Leeds, 1896-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to Bp. of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop of Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYERS, CECIL GEORGE LEWIS.—B. 1903; ed. St. Paul's Sch. and Oxford; classical schol., Balliol Coll., 1921-26; 1st cls. classical moda., 1923; 1st cls., lit. hum., 1925; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 21st Oct., 1925; pvt. sec. to perm. under-secr., for Doms. affrs., 14th Nov., 1928.

SYKES, GEORGE ROGNVALD.—Cadet, S. Stintms., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attached censor's office, Singapore; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1916; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apr., 1917; asst. registrar imports and exports (war trade dept.) Singapore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1920; supernumy, offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies and asst. prot. of Chinese, Malacca, June, 1921.

SYMES, LIEUT.-COL. SIR GEORGE STEWART, K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps) Aden, 1903-04 (deeps., D.S.O.); attd., Egyptian Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expedn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gen.-gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; Sudan War (3 timesment in depts., med., brevet lt.-col.); attd., staff of high comnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd., miny. of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov't., Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admst. gov't., Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept., 1927; accredited rep. to mandates comm., June, 1926; res. and commd.-in-ch., Aden, 1928.

SYMONT, SIR JOSIAH HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1846; mem. of H. of Assem. S. Australia, 1881-1887; atty.-gen., 1881; Q.C. 1881; representative to Aus. fed. conven., 1897-8, and chrmn. of its judiciary comtees; pres. of fed. league of S. Aust.; pres. of Commonwealth league; senr. for S. Aust., Commonwealth parl., 1901-1913; atty.-gen., C. of A., 1904-5; has ret. from politics.
SYMONTETTE, W. J.—B. 1878; pub. schl. teacher, 18th Apr., 1898; 4th divn. comnr., 11th June, 1921; ag. oh. clk. recvr. gen. in treas. dept., 6th Nov., 1922; confirmed in appt., 4th Feb., 1924.

SYMONS, CLAUDE TREBINE.—B. 1879; B.A. Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, June, 1912; ag. govt. analyst, in addn., Dec., 1916; govt. analyst, June, 1919; ag. regsr., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TACHE, JOSEPH DE LABROQUIERE.—B. 1858; ed. St. Hyacinthe Sem. and Laval Univ.; notary public, 1881; priv. sec. to Hon. J. A. Chapleau, premier of Quebec, 1880-82; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov.
