(De Lisles), capt., 4th Batt., "The King's" (Liverpool Regt. (S. Africa), 1899-1902; supt., R.E. tel. constrn. party, S. Nigeria, 1903-05; seconded for serv. under C.O. as asst. supt., tels., Uganda and E.A.P., 21st Dec., 1905; asst. P.M.G. and asst. tel. engr. for the Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1912; transf., dep. P.M.G., Gold Coast, 12th Mar., 1913; ag. P.M.G. on various occasions; major (2nd in commd.), 13th (Service) Batt., "The King's" (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; censor, Accra, 1915-16; special military service, Palame, Togoland, Feb., 1916; served, headqrs. supply staff, min. of munitions, Aug., 1916 to Jan., 1917; ag. engineer-in-chie, postal tels., Gold Coast, Nov., 1923 to May, 1924.

WALSH, DUDLEY J. C.—B. 1896; ed. Merchant Taylors Schol.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-16; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa, 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr. Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

WALSH, GEORGE.—B. 1884; Imp. cust. surv., Apr., 1906; collr. of cust., Mombasa, May, 1913; seconded to occupied territory of German E. Africa to organise cust. surv., with rank of capt., 1916-17; in charge of cust. dept. of occupied area, German E. Africa, Jan. to July, 1917; dep. comanr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1920; comanr., cust., Kenya and Uganda and mem., leg. coun., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WALTER, A., F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comanr., 1909; census comanr., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imgrts. and poor law comanr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs', confco., 1926; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY.—B.A. Cantab.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., 15th Dec., 1911; att. Galle Kach., May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1917; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaittvu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Regalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matare, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929.

WALTON, B. S.—B.A. (Oxon.).—cadet, S. Stltmts. civ. serv., 29th Nov., 1913; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 23rd Mar., 1914; asst. censor, 23rd Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., Singapore V.R., 28th Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Singapore, 2nd Nov., 1916; supernumerary offr., cls. V., 25th Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), 17th Dec., 1917; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Penang, 5th Aug., 1919; supernumy offr., cls. IV., 29th Nov., 1920.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas., gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high comanr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, KT. BACH. (1925).—B. 1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "K," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 13th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. admstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926.
WARD, C. E.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 7th Aug., 1909; dist. comanr., Jan., 1918.
WARD, ESPEINE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast;

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served in France with Civil Service Rifles, 16th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lancs. Fusiliers, May, 1915; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, and served with them in France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. at taking of Jerusalem and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Batang Padang, Sept., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Mar., 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, May, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; asst. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Relantian, Apr., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; ag. supt. lands, Kota Bharu, Feb., 1925; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1927; ag. asst. adviser, lands, Kedah, July, 1927; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schol., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. hona., B.A., mod. hist., 1923; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDE, HARRY MONTAGU JOHN.—B. 1873; ed. at Epsom Coll., entd. Crown Agents' office, Sept., 1892; asst. head of miscellaneous dept., Oct., 1900; chief cashier, July, 1914; head of gen. stores dept., Dec., 1920; head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; head, gen. stores dept., Oct., 1924.

WARDLE, BRIAN.—B. 1891; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; Indian pol., 1911-22; King's Pol. med.; supt. in charge, C.I.D., Zanzibar, 1924; immigr. offr., May to Nov., 1925, Aug., 1926 to Feb., 1928 and from Aug., 1928.
WARDLE, D. S.—Temp. asst. treas., Kenya, June, 1920; acct. med. dept., Apr., 1921; asst. treas., July, 1923.
WARNE, OSWALD HORNBY.—B. 1891; ed. Rossal Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxford; lieut., South Staffordshire Regt.; asst. dist. commnr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

WARNER, BRODRICK ASHTON.—B. 1888; ed. Winchester Coll.; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Prot., 1912; lab. commnr., 1922; 1st grade administrative offr., 1923; senr. asst. sec., 1924; late capt., 5th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, EDGAR HERBERT, M.B.E. (1880).—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customs, Nyasaland Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also immigr. offr., 1913; principal immigr. offr., May, 1914; asst contr., cust., Apr., 1920; ag. compr., cust., July, 1922; compr., cust., Mar., 1925.

WARREN, ERNEST, D.Sc. (Lond.)—Demonstrator of zoology at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1894; life mem. of convocation of the Univ. of Lond., 1898; asst. lecturers and museum curator at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1899; elected fellow of Univ. Coll., 1898; asst. prof. of zoology at Univ. Coll., 1900; mem. of the faculty of science, Univ. of Lond., 1902; dir. of Natal govt. museum, 1st Feb., 1903.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; stock impr., Basutoland, 1914; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19.

WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, C.M.G. (1908); Assoc. Inst. C.E., F.R.G.S., F.Met. Soc.—B. 1861; asset. survr., Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1878; dist. survr., 1st Oct., 1879; office astt. to survr.-gen., 16th June, 1885; astt. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; survr.-gen., 23rd June, 1904; M.L.C., Ceylon, 1907-8; ret. 1910.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ. Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice ment. in despa.; French Croix de Guerre; astt. dist. commnr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. commnr., 1st Apr., 1924.

WATERALL, LEONARD SAXTON, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. class, tripes, 1907; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1911; cadet, N.E. Rhodesia, 1908; astt. native commnr., 1911; native commnr., 1912; passed law and higher Chiwembe; ag. Br. vice cons., Katanga, 1914; inspr., Rhodesian natives, Katanga, 1914; war serv., commr., Rifle Brigade, 1914; France and Belgium, 1915-16; Egypt, 1917; O.C., prisoner of war camps, Palestine, 1918; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1918; ag. dist. polit. offr., 1919-20; passed higher Swahili, 1922; law, 1924; dist. offr., 1924; dep. lab. commnr., 1926; ag. lab. commnr., 1926 and 1929.

WATERLAND, HAROLD.—B. 1888; ed. privately, and Harper-Adams Agr. Coll.; dipl. agr.; M.R.A.S.E., sci. mast., govt. gram. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1911; censor, 1914; astt. chem., Impl. dept., agr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1914; sen. astt., agr. dept., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1916; ag. dir., agr., various periods, 1916, 1918, 1920; del., Imp. Entomol. Conf., 1920; agr. dept., Zanzibar, May, 1922; ag. astt. dir., agr., in 1926, 1928 and 1929; astt. dir., agr., July, 1929.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaf Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Calais Coll., Cambridge; maths. schol., Calais Coll., 1899; maths. tripes, 1902; law tripes, 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922.

WATERS, BERNARD DAVID.—B. 1879; telegraphist, Cape Colony, May, 1902 to Aug., 1905; postal astt., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1905; ch. astt., Apr., 1912; sec., post office, May, 1913; ag. P.M.G., Jan., 1925 to May, 1926; dep. P.M.G., Nov., 1926.

WATERS, HORACE FRANK, M.C., V.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Perse Gram. Schl., Camb.; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, Oct., 1908; seconded for mily. serv., May, 1914; 2nd lieut., 2/16th batt., London Regt., Feb., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1915; temp. capt., mach. gun corps., Jan., 1916; temp. major, July, 1916; instr., genl. hd.-qrs., mach. gun schl., B.E.F., May, 1917; ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1917; corps mach. gun offr., VIII, corps hd.-qrs., B.E.F., May, 1918; demob., Mar., 1919; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Sept., 1921 and Decorn., Sept., 1926; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Aug., 1922; ag. senr. ex. engrnr., Kinta, Jan., 1924; lieut., M.S.V.R., June, 1924; ag. ch. hydrau. engrnr., F.M.S., July, 1926; senr. ex. engrnr., Oct., 1926; capt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engrnr., Kinta, Jan., 1927; ag. state engrnr., Perak, Jan. to June, 1928; state engrnr., F.M.S., 1929.

WATERS, THOMAS JOSEPH.—B. 1883; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll., Cork (B. engrngr.); G.S.O., 2nd grade, R.E., W.O., 1916; astt. engr., Baro-Kano Rly., Aug. 1909; ag. ch. survr., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1912; astt. survr.-gen., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; dep. survr.-gen., May, 1922; survr.-gen., Dec., 1925.

WATKINS, OSCAR FERRIS, C.B.E. (1918), D.S.O. (1918).—Ed., Marlborough and All Souls, Oxford, B.A., M.A., 1910; served with Oxfordshire Lt. Infy. (43rd) during S. African War, Dec., 1899 to 1901 (Queen's Med. and 4 clasps); S. African constab., Dec., 1902 to Apr., 1904; col. sec.'s office, Transvaal, May, 1904 to Oct., 1907; sec. to Pretoria sewage commr. and govt. laboratories commr.; astt. dist. commnr., Br. E. Africa, 16th Jan., 1908; dist. commnr., Apr., 1914; seconded for Coast Land arbit., Sept., 1913 to Aug., 1914; seconded as intell. offr. to organise carrier transport D.A.D.T.; E. African Forces, 1915; dir., mil. labour, 1916; ag. ch. native commnr., Aug., 1920 to Aug., 1921; offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1920 to Feb., 1922; mem., land tenure commr., 1920; mem., central bd. of health, 1921; mem., native punishments commr., 1921; chmn., labour bureau commr., 1921; dep. ch. native commnr., Aug., 1921; senr. commnr., Jan., 1923.
WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Schl.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1900; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; Contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtees., 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comtees., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., commr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, registr. of shipping, Apr., 1923.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Cik., gov. of Zululand's office, 1896; ag. sec. for Zululand at different periods, 1896; clk., gov't. office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf'd. Cape civ. serv. and apptd. clk., gov't. office, Mar., 1901; ch. clk., July, 1904; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admatg. govt., major-gen. R. S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scoball, K.C.V.O., Dec., 1909; astt. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902, B.Sc. 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Singapore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr. of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923.
WATSON, P. O. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.R.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec., and partly head of depts. of post office, telegraphs, educn., rly., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comttee., Diocesan Synod, 1910; ag. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. and the Coronation, 1911; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1913; hon. priv. sec. to gov., 1916; asst. priv. sec. to gov., 1918; hon. corres. sec., Royal Col. Inst.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1889; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engnr. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr. (mech.), 1928.

WATT, ALEXANDER STUART.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad., Edin. Instit., Blair Lodge and Edin. Univ.; writer to the signet, 1909; capt., 5th Batt. Royal Scots; asst. dist. comsnnr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnnr., Dec., 1915; attached G. Coast Regt., 1915; with E. Africa Expd. Force, July, 1916.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commndg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATT, RT. HON. WM. ALEXANDER.—P.O. (1920); M.H.A.; treas. of Victoria Jan., 1909; premier and treas., 1912, and again in Dec., 1913; resig. premiership to enter H. of R., C. of A., to which he was elected in Sep., 1914; min. for works and rly., 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; treas., 27th Mar., 1918; min. for trade and cust., 13th Dec., 1918; acting prime min., 1919; resig. office, 1920; speaker, H. of R., 1923; ret. from politics, 1929.

WATTS, BERTRAM T., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schol. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1905-08; inspr. Egyptian govt. survey, 1913; on sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir., sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regnr. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919, 1923 and 1926; regnr. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish; title altered to dir., land registration and surveys, July, 1927.

WATTS, CLIVE.—2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acmnt., treas., Cyprus, 1920; acmnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; passed exam. in Greek, 1921; ag. asst. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1926 and 1927; ag. asst. sec., col. sec's. office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan, 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., June to Oct., 1928.

WATTS, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1917), C.M.G. (1904), D.Sc., F.C.S., F.I.C.—B. 1859; analytical chemist, Antigua, Jan., 1889; mem. legis. couns., 1897; analytical and agricultural chemist, Jamaica, May, 1898; Leeward Is., May, 1899 (in connection with the Imperial dept. of agriculture for the W. Indies); office subsequently made that of govt. chemist and asupt. of agric. for the Leeward Is.; mem. exec. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1899; mem. exec. couns., Antigua, Dec., 1903; Imperial comsnr. of agric. in W. Indies, 1909; prin., W. Indian Agrl. Coll., 1921; prin., emeritus, 1924; author of numerous reports and papers on W. Indian agriculture, etc.

WAYLAND, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Schol., City of London Coll., R. Coll. of Science; nat. sci. studentship (C.L.C.), 1906; nat. schl. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.I.M.M., F.G.S., London, etc.; asst. min. survr., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. in 25 Tunelling Coy., R.E., as forward demolition reconnaissence offr. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Boring Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir., geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1926; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. pvte. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29; passed exam. in mod. Greek, June, 1928.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVELYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's medal with 5 claps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; asst. dist. comsnnr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1910; ag. dist. comsnnr., 1914; dist. comsnnr., 1919; ag. prov. comsnnr., 1924; lab. comsnnr., Uganda, 1st Jan., 1925; ag. prov. comsnnr., Sept., 1926; prov. comsnnr., 2nd grade, 1927; prov. comsnnr., 1st grade, 1927.
WEAVING, L. A.—Asst. dist. comsnnr., Kenya, June, 1921.
WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1928), M.C.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., 1st Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, 20th June, 1923.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Innns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921.

WEBB, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1888; ed. Christ Coll., Blackheath and Guy's Hosp.—M.B. (hons.); B.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda, 1913; sp. serv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. capt., Uganda med. serv., 1914-16; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1918-19; senr. med. offr., 1923; spec. offr., med. supt., Mulago hosp., 1926; dep. dir. med. serv., Uganda, 1928.

WEBBER, ARTHUR FREDERICK CLARENCE.—B. 1873; ed. Merton Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Tem., Feb., 1896; ag. stip. mag., 1898, and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Essequibo, Oct., 1900; ditto, Whim Correntyne, July, 1902; ditto, Berbice Judicial dist., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1907, and in July, 1909; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Sept., 1909.
WEBBER, OSCAR WM.—B. 1871; clk. asst., prisons office, B. Guiana, 1887; 5th cls. clk., P.O., 1889; transfd. to secretariat, 1896; 4th cls.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admnstr., Oct., 1916; adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. comsnr., Mwanza dist., Feb., Nov. to Nov., 1923; ag. senr. comsnr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. comsnr., Jan., 1926; ag. prov. comsnr., E. Prov. Mar., 1926; off. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. comsnr., Feb., 1928; prov. comsnr., junr. grade, May, 1929.

WEBSTER, G. W., M.B.E. (1913).—Ed. at Rossall; served with Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 23rd Aug., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1918; senr. res., 1920.
WEBSTER, H. G.—Asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1911; ag. addtl. asst. col. sec. in addition to own duties, July, 1912; ag. asst. col. sec., July, 1912; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordce.", Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordce.", Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1916; asst. settm. offr., July, 1920; ag. settm. offr., Mar. to Nov., 1921 and from Apr., 1922 to June, 1924; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. gov't. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; gov't. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929.

WEDGE, CAPT. CRIL NORMAN.—B. 1896; schol. med. hist., St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge, Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., Mar., 1915; served in Gallipoli (Suvla Bay), 1915; "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory Meds.; seconded 1st K.A.R., 1918; served in Portuguese E. Africa and Nyasaland, 1918-20; capt. and adjt., 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-23; pay and qtrmr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, January, 1923.

WEERAKOON, WILLIAM AUGUSTUS.—B. 1878; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst. to gov't. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1900; Batticaloa, Nov., 1901; Kurunegala, May, 1902; Jaffna, Mar., 1904; Ratnapura, May, 1904; pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1908; Point Pedro, Aug., 1908; addtnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Negombo, June, 1916; pol. mag., Jaffna, Aug., 1916; office asst. to gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1916; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1918; office asst. to gov't. agt., W. Prov., July, 1920.

WEGERER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Crossley Sch., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rly., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S. rly., 1922; works man., loco. dept., F.M.S. rly., July, 1928.

WEIGALL, Lieut.-Col. Sir William Ernest George Archibald, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Herefordshire Div., Lincoln, 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem. of select comm. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., federation of county agric. comittees; J.P., Kent and Lincs.; gov't., S. Australia, 27th Mar., 1920; assumed govt., 9th June, 1920; resig., 1922.

WEISBECKER, ARNOLD.—Clk., census dept., Cape Prov., 17th Apr., 1891; P.W.D., 29th Feb., 1892; admistr.'s office (Cape Prov.), 1st July, 1910; ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1911; ch. local govt. inspr. and ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prov. secy. and ch. local govt. inspr., 1st Sept., 1922.

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripos.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1917; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1917; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, May, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu, May, 1920; ditto, Kuala Langat, May, 1922; mag., Seremban, July, 1922; ag. state treas., Perak, Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, July, 1924; ag. state treas., Perak, Aug., 1924; temp. ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, in addn., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Larut and regular, titles, Perak, 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, October, 1927; ag. pub. trustee and offr. admistr., F.M.S., Oct., 1928; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929.
WELBY, H. E.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.
WELCH, THOMAS BURGES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B.B.S., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1881; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlement, Trinidad, 23rd Sept., 1925.

WELLINGTON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. THOMAS HENRY SPROTT, M.A., D.D.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonial (1st cls.), and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitate), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1879-1882; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1882-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELLINGTON, ARTHUR ROBERTS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.F.H. (Camb.).—B. 1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1906; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, 13th May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., 5th Mar., 1919; offr. del. to 4th cong., Far Eastern Asso., Trop. Med., Java, Aug., 1921; also offi. del., 5th cong., S'pore, Sept., 1923; offi. del., L. of N. Eastern Health Confce., S'pore, Feb., 1925; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1925; offi. del., 6th cong., F.E.A.T.M., Japan, Sept., 1925; offi. rep., L. of N. interchange of health offrs. in Japan, Korea and Manchuria, Nov. 1925; offi. mem. advisory coun., L. of N. Eastern Health Bureau, S'pore, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1926; offi. del. for F.M.S. and S.S. at Internat. Health Confce., Melbourne, Dec., 1926; on offi. duty, Panama, Feb., 1927; ch. health offr., F.M.S., Feb., 1927; offi. del., Far Eastern Asso., Trop. Med., India, Dec., 1927; offi. mem., L. of N. Eastern Health Bureau, Delhi, Dec., 1927; ag. prin. med. offr., June, 1928; dir., med. and sanry. services Hong Kong, 1929.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B.1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; professional assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1921; law adviser, 1926.

WELSH, GORDON JAMES.—B. 1894; ed. Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schl., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pvt., 1/4th Batt. The Buffs and 2nd lieut., The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; clk. to gov't., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. clk., Apr., 1923; seconded to Somaliland as asst. sec. to gov't., Mar., 1929; ag. dist. commnr., Burao dist., Aug., 1929.

WELSH, WILLIAM THOMSON.—B. 1873; clk. to mag., Cape Town, Nov., 1892; Bedford, Dec., 1892; Kimberley, Jan., 1894; Klipdam, April, 1895; Barkly West, July, 1895; Beaconfield, Jan., 1896; Prince Albert, Mar., 1896; Jansenville, May, 1896; asst. regist., high ct., Kimberley, July, 1896; clk. to mag., Victoria East, Aug., 1900; asst. reg. mag., Paarl, Aug., 1901; King Williams Town, Aug., 1904; Middelriff, Aug., 1907; reg. mag., Libode, July, 1908; Mqanduli, May, 1910; Kokstad, Feb., 1916; asst. chief mag., Transkeian Territories, and reg. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1919; ch. mag., Transkeian Territories, Sept., 1920.
WERTH, ALBERTUS JOHANNES.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher at Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-19; M.P. for Kroonstad, 1921-26; admstr., S.W. Africa, Apr., 1926.

WHEATLEY, MAJOR H. H., O.B.E., M.C.—Served with Royal Engineers in France, 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); asst. to ch. engr. and sec. to gov't., public wks. dept., Iraq, 1920; asst. adviser to min. of communications and wks., 1st Jan., 1921; adviser to min. of communications and wks., 9th Apr., 1922.

WHREBELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; joined Irish Guards, Oct., 1914; transf. to 1st Guards Brig. Machine Gun Coy., Sept., 1915; served throughout Great War with Guards Divn., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in desps., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W. M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; asst. comdt., pol., Bahamas, 21st Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., 20th Aug., 1926.
WHEELER, CAPT. RICHARD CHAMBERLAIN, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, July, 1913; senior veterinary officer, Apr., 1920.

WHINERAY, SIDNEY BUCKINGHAM.—B. 1879; ast. acct., Baro Kano rly., N. Nigeria, 1911; Nigerian rly., 1913; sp. investgn., acct., min. of munitions, 1918; ast. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., 1923 and 1928-29.

WHISKARD, GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, C.B. (1923).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wedham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; ast. prvc. sec. and subsequently priv. prvc. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; ast. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; ast. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., Oversea Settlement C'tee., 4th Sept., 1929.
WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B. 1899; entered C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 1917; on military service, Jan., 1918, to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

WHITE, ALEXANDER HAROLD, O.B.E. (1889); ast. collr. (now ast. dist. commnr.), Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. 2nd ast. sec. and clk. of coun., June, 1915; ag. 1st ast. sec., Oct. to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., His Highness' st. for Zanzibar, 1918 (Brilliant Star, 4th cls., July, 1919); ag. dist. commnr., Feb. to July, 1919; ast. sec., German E. Africa, provisional admin., July, 1919; dist. political offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. mag., Dar-es-Salam, Apr. to July, 1922; seconded for sp. serv. as Br. commnr., Anglo-Belgian undy. commn. (Ruanda-Urundi), Aug., 1922 to Aug., 1926; prov. commnr., Jan., 1926.

WHITE, ARTHUR V.—B. 1871; ed. Woodstock Pub. and High Schls., and Univ. of Toronto (M.E.); engr. with several prominent firms; lect. in mech. drawing and machine design, Toronto Tech. Schl.; later with dept. of pub. wks., Can.; consulting engrnr., Commn. of Conservation, Can., until its abolition, 1921; consulting engrnr., representing Can. to the internat. joint commn., Lake of the Woods investigation under the boundary waters treaty, 1911; consulting engrnr. to Hydro-electric Commn., Ontario, 1923; has written extensively on water power, elec. development and fuel supply.

WHITE, JAMES, F.R.G.S., Grad. R.M.C., Kingston, Canada.—B. 1863; entd. civ. serv., Canada, 1884; geographer and ch. draughtsman, geog. surv., 1894; ch. geographer, dep. of Interior, 1899; sec., Commr.of Conservation, 1909, and sec. and chmn. until its abolition, 1921; engaged on sp. work in connection with Labrador boundary dispute.

WHITE, W. ARTHUR.—Apptd. after open compet. exam. for home civil serv. to P.O., Jan., 1890; 2nd div. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., July, 1892; clk., controller's office, Mar., 1896; supt. of registrtn., G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1903; ag. supt. of mails, Oct., 1903; acctnt., postal dept., F.M.S., 1905; ag. dir., posts and telegraphs, F.M.S., 19th May, 1910 to 27th Aug., 1911, and 13th May, 1915 to 2nd Jan., 1916; sen. ast. auditor, external audit, S. Stltmz. and F.M.S., 1st July, 1917; seconded as collr. of war tax, Singapore, July to Nov., 1917; dir., external audit, S. Stltmz., and F.M.S., 12th May, 1919; ag. audr.-gen., S. Stltmz., in addition, 29th May, 1920 to 10th Nov., 1921; audr.-gen., 14th Aug., 1923.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Thomas, P.C. (1920); K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls., Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law school; apptd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.

WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mil'y. serv.; 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Min'y. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; apptd. cler. offr., O.O., 24th Apr., 1922.

WHITEHEAD, FRANK EDRED, O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); certif., Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med.—B. 1880; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; late hon. surg. County Hosp., Huntingdon; med. offr., Sierra Leone, May, 1913; Cameroons Exped. Force, Aug., 1914 to Feb., 1916; med offr., Somaliland, June, 1916; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Nyasaland, July, 1922; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1922; dir., med. and sany. services, 1923.
WHITEHEAD, LOUIS HAROLD.—B. 1871; chief clerk to atty-gen., Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1905.

WHITEHORN, ROGER HERBERT, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; attd. col. sec.'s office, 12th Dec., 1911; attd. Colombokach., Aug., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1913; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th ast. col. sec., Sept., 1914; on military duty as ast. censor, Nov., 1914; ag. ast. govt. agt., Colomba, Apr., 1916; 4th ast. col. sec., Aug., 1916; ag. third ast. col. sec., Oct., 1916; priv. sec. to gov. in addn. Nov., 1916; on military duty, 1918; extra ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1920; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1920; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., 5th-23rd Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1922; ag. ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1923; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1924; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, Dec., 1924; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1927; chmn., munia. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1927.

WHITEHOUSE, BERTRAM REGINALD.—B. 1891; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1913; cadet, S. Stlnta. civ. ser., Feb., 1915, and lent to Johore; attached to censor's office, Singapore, till Apr., 1918; granted permission to join H.M. Forces, Apr., 1918; attached to land office, Johore, Sep., 1919; ag. supt. of Indian immigr., Nov., 1919; ag. collr. of land rev., Johore Bahru, June, 1920; offr., cls. IV., 9th Jan., 1922.

WHITLERY, MICHAEL HENRY.—B. 1872; ed. at Blundell's Schol. and King's Coll., London; jun. offr., Selangor, 1896; dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., 1906; legal adviser, Johore, 1910; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1913; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., 1916; puline judge, S.S. and jud. comsnr., F.M.S., 1918; ag. senr. puline judge, S.S., 1921; ag. puline judge, Penang, 1st Jan., 1922; ag. senr. puline judge, Penang, June, 1922; atty.-gen., S.S., 1925.

WHITTALL, LIEUT.-COL. PERCIVAL FREDERICK.—B. 1877; ed. Felsted; joined army, 1896; lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1902; served in S. African war, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); lieut., G. Coast Regt., 1907; reed. thanks of S. of B. for services during outbreak of yellow fever at Secondee, 1910; ast. dist. comsnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1912; released for service in European War, 1915-1919 (D.S.O. and one bar, French Croix de Guerre, 1914-16 Star (ment. 4 times despa.); dist. comsnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1918; dep. prov. comsnr., 1927; prov. comsnr., 1928.
WHITTING, CHARLES EDWARD JEWEL.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.

WHITTLE, REGINALD ALFRED, M.C.—B. 1897 ed. Manchester Grammar Schol. and Queen's Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. math. mods., 1919; B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served in France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; ast. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 1921; ast. sec., 1922.

WHYBRO, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schol., London Univ., Schol. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1923; law tripes, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WHYTE-SMITH, THOMAS STODDART.—B. 1883; ed. Merchiston Castle Schol. and Edinburgh Univ.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., Royal Scots, 1915; lieut., 1916; capt., K.A.R., 1918; 2nd ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ag. ast. land offr., Mar. to Nov., 1924 and Sept., 1926; ast. crown solr., 1926; ag. mag., Kowloon, 1929.

WICKHAM, CAPT. DENIS HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; Connaught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., France, 1915-18; mentd. in desps.; transf'd. to Queen's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th K.A.R. Kenya, 1922-26; admstv. offr., Kenya col.; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

WICKRAMASINGHE, SENA PANDUKABHAYA, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; attd. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1926; office ast., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1927; ditto, Point Pedro, Apr., 1929.

WICKREMESINGHE, CYRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Apr., 1912; Matara Kachcheri, Oct., 1912; office ast. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, and Avissawella, Mar., 1921; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; ast. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928.

WIDDUP, CHARLES PONSONBY.—Clerical ast., treasy., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub-inspr. of police, 1904; qualified as musketry instr., Hythe, 1905; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen., B. Guiana, 16th Dec., 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces, Mar. to Oct., 1924 and from Aug., 1926 to Jan., 1927.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Apr., 1918; ag. res. comsnr., 1926.

WIGHTON, ALEXANDER FITCHIE, Mem., Transvaal Soc'y, of Acctnts.—Joined Impl. military rlys., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. acctnt.'s dept., June, 1903; acctnt., engrnr.'s dept., Feb., 1904; ch. clk., ditto, July, 1908; inspr., dept. of audr., S. African rlys. and harbrs., Apr., 1910; ch. inspr., asst. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept. Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; ch. inspr. (rlys. and harbr.), cont. and audit office, July, 1916.

WIGLEY, WILFRED MURRAY.—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical apptmts. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, pulse judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Comm'l Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmichling including Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rlys. and harbrs. bd., 1924; pub. debt. comsmr., 1925; admstr. O.F.S., Mar., 1929.

WILFORD, THOMAS MASON.—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-25; chmn., Wellington harbr. bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; since leader of opposition and liberal party; high comsmr. for N.Z. in London, 1929.

WILKIE, DAVID.—Ed. at Christ's Hosp., London; S. African campaign, 1900-1; pay and acctng. branch, S.A.C., 1902; clk. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1906; ch. clk., gov.'s off., 1907; clk. of exec. coun., 1909; gov.-gen.'s off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to tender bd., 1911; ag. contr. of supplies, 1916; gov't. buyer, 1920.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER (Lieut., R.N.V.R.)—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war service (Navy), Aug., 1914, to June, 1919; gunnery offr. from July, 1918; asst. dist. comsmr., G. Coast, July, 1919; ag. dist. comsmr., 10th Sept. to Nov., 1919.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD, M.B.E. (1922).—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transf'd. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; clk. to finance and concessions comm., 1916; clk. for legal instruments, Sept., 1921; staff offr., 1st April, 1922; librarian, 26th Feb., 1923.

WILKINSON, H.S.—Clk., high comsmr.'s office, S. Africa, 1899; clk., polit. sec.'s office, Transvaal, 1900; acctnt. to sec., Transvaal adminstr., 1901; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1902; controller of stores, educn. dept., 1902; acctnt., health dept., 1904; sec., civ. serv. bd., 1906; distributor of stamps, treasv., 1908; distributor of stamps, dept. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasv., 1916; ch. clk., treasv., 1925.
WILKINSON, RICHARD CLARY.—B. 1905; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Nov., 1928.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham School and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-11; joined West African med. staff, 10th Jan., 1912.

WILCOCKS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 14th Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice mention in despatches); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; (placed on R. of O.); A.D.C., Kenya, 19th Feb., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., 1st May, 1928.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLETT, THOMAS GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1908; pol. mag., Chilaw, Dec., 1909; addl. pol. mag., Galie, Matara, Tangalla, Hambantota and Balapitiya, July, 1910; pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Aug., 1913; asst. land settm't. offr., and special offr. under waste land ordce., June, 1914; spec. comsmr. in connection with the riots, June, 1915; asst. land settm't. offr., Sept., 1915; on military duty, 1918; asst.-govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1920; pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Jan., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Dec., 1924; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 16th to 27th Jan., 1926; asst. stlitm. offr., Feb., 1929.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B.—Med. offr., K.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; asst. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lancs.; inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rlys., 1898; Crown Agent's office, 1900; asst. man., loco. wks., Robt. Stephenson, Darlington, 1901; dep. ch. inspr. engr., Crown Agts., 1904; staff capt. W.O., 1915; section dir., Miny. of Munitions, 1916; major, R.E., D.A.D., W.O., 1918; tech. head of engrn. inspection dept., O.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspr. engr., 1922; vice president Inst. of Loco. Engrs.
WILLIAMS, D. L. GWINNE.—B. 1886; solr., sup't., Eng., 1914; served European War, 1915-18; ot. martial offr., 1918; staff capt. (legal), 1919; conveyancer and regitr. of titles, Uganda, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BRIDGEWATER.—B.1886; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Brusas, Mar., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, S.S., June, 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1914; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Sept., 1918; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Coast, Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Nov., 1921; collr., land rev., Seremban, Mar., 1922; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ag. collr., land rev., Seremban, Nov., 1925; ag. collr., estate duty, F.M.S., Dec., 1926; pub. trustee, F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1927; offr., assigne, F.M.S., May., 1927; offr., cls. I B. legal advr. and judic. comsmr., Kelantan, June, 1928.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD LLOYD.—B. 1875; ed. at Brecon and Victoria Univ. (Univ. Coll., Liverpool); indentured for three years to the water engrn. of Liverpool; student of the instit. of civ. engrns.; contractor's engr. on rly. construction, G.W.R.; res. engr. on waterworks construction, N. Wales county asylum; contractor's sub-agt. and engrnr. on rly. construction, G.W.R., served with 29th I.Y. (Denbighshire Hussars), South Africa, 1901-1; asst. engrnr. on rly. survey, Gold Coast and Ashanti, 1901-2; asst. surveyor of mines, G. Coast, 1902-3; ch. surveyor, 1904-5; contractor's agt., waterworks construction for Liverpool corporation, 1905-6; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 9th June, 1906; ag. dep. D.P.W., in 1909, 1910, 1911 and 1912.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREGEAR.—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, S. Stltmts. civ. ser., 28th Nov., 1913; attached to col. secs. office, 26th Dec., 1913; ag. supt., govt. printing office, 12th Mar., 1914; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, 1st Sept., 1915; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 19th Oct., 1917; supervmry. offr., cls. IV, S. Stltmts., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., "B," June, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. "A," Dec., 1921, Jan. and Apr., 1924; ag. 1st asst. sec. B, Jan. and Mar., 1924; ag. clk. of couns., Jan., 1924; ag. 1st asst. sec. A, May, 1924 and Sept., 1927; ag., commr., trade and cust., Johore, Aug., 1925; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Oct., 1925; ag. stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Apr., 1927.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; asst. to sec., for Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. dist. offr., New Territories, Aug., 1928.

WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. asst. audr., July, 1924; ag. dep. audr., on various occasions from 1926.
WILLIAMS, G. A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.C.R.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. commission, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1921.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT NEVILLE, D.S.O., V.D.—Cape civ. serv., surv. office, 1892; dept. of agr., 1893; prin. clk., 1906; sh. clk., 1909; sh. clk., Union dept. of agr., 1912; ag. under-seer., 1919; confirmed, 1920; served, Bechuanaland Rebellion, 1897 (med. and clasp); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); served with Union troops, S.W. Africa, 1914-15; A.A. and Q.M.G., 3rd divn., E. African Expedy. Force, 1917-19 (D.S.O., 3 times men. in desps.); sec. for agr., 1st Aug., 1926; del. to Imp. Agrl. Confec., London, 1927.

WILLIAMS, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough, McGill Univ., Montreal and Cambridge; colonial exhibitioner, Trinity Coll., 1924-26; 2nd cls., nat. sci. trips, pt. I., 1927; 2nd cls. pt. II., 1928, B.A. 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 17th Oct., 1929.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1886; ed. Lansing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th Mar., 1919; priv., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, P. MILES.—B. 1890; ed. Pontypridd County Schl. and Ravensburg High Schl., Würtemberg, Germany; R.F.C., 1915-17; disabled; clk., Miny. of Labour, Bristol, Feb., 1918 to May, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th May, 1922; seconded to Empire Marketing Bd., 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE.—B. 1889; ed. at Harrow (head of the schl. 1907-08) and at Caius Coll., Camb. (schol. and Sayer schol.), 1st cls. classical tripos, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Feb., 1915; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1916; asst. sec. to rec., Perak, Feb., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1920; ag. temp. as dist. offr., Batang Padang, July-Aug., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1921; 2nd mag. Penang, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. advr., Perlis, May, 1925, to May, 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, July, 1929.

WILLIAMS, R. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Schl., Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hons., Science Tripos; Royal Schl. of Mines, A.R.S.M., M. Inst. M.M., 1st cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; asst. res., N. Nigeria, May., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911; contr., mines, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; commr., of mines, 1929.
WILLIAMS, SIDNEY BRUC.—B. 1880; S.A. constab., 1901; Swaziland pol., 1908; clk., 1911; R.J.P., 1916; dep. asst. comsrr., 1928.
WILLIAMS, THOMAS ALFRED.—B. 1875; acctng. clk., Basutoland, 1906; acctng., 1913; finan. sec., 1916; transf'd. Bech. Prot. as audr., H.C. Territories, July, 1927.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1880; ed. Carnarvon County Schl., Univ. Coll., N. Wales and Univ. of Liverpool (engnr. dept.); attd. Portsmouth dockyard, 1909; sent to Hong Kong for electrification H.M. naval dy., May, 1909; ch. asst. engnr., Shanghai munic. elec. dept., 1911; engnr.-in-ch. and man., Penang munic. electy. supply and tramways undertakings, 1920; engnr. and man., Penang Hill rlya., Oct., 1923; dir., elect. dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1927.

WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treas'y., Transvaal, July, 1904; senr. clk., Jan., 1919; prin. clk., Apr., 1921; sec., cabinet comtees on expenditure, 1922-24; ch. clk., estimates, June, 1929.

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (er. 1924); 1st Baron of Ratton (cr. 1910); Freeman Freeman-Thomas, G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1896; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jun. lord of treas., 1905-12; J.P.: gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanry; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; gov'r-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926.

WILLIS, HARRY GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. Marlborough Coll.; apptd. to N.E. Rhodesia, Oct., 1900; asst. native comsrr., Jan., 1901; civ. comsrr., Sept., 1903; native comsrr., Nov., 1904; ret. owing to ill-health, Oct., 1906; rejoined as clk., mag.'s ct., Livingstone, N.W.R., Dec., 1907; native comsrr., Oct., 1908; ag. asst. mag., N.R., Sept., 1912-Mar., 1913; asst. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., Sept., 1913-Mar., 1914; ag. mag., Nov., 1923-May, 1924; mag., 31st May, 1924; J.P.

WILLIS, MAURICE A.—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf'd., O.O., May, 1924.

WILLIS, NORMAN EDWARD OMMANNEY.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton; entd. crown agent's office, 1st Jan., 1891; asst. cashier, 4th Jan., 1899; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1902; acctnt. and dep. chief elk., 9th Jan., 1913; head of gen. dept. and ch. elk., 16th Nov., 1920.

WILLIS, STANLEY SCOTT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Lawrence Coll.; P.A.S.I., 1912; staff surrv., Kenya, 1914; milly. serv., 1915-19; staff surrv., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; on Anglo-Belgian bdy. comm., 1922-24.

WILLIS, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. London; elk., treasy. and cust. house, Turks and Caicos Is., Jan., 1925; ag. asst. treas., postmr., cellr. of cust., regiar. of shipping and gen. man., savings bank, May, 1928 to Aug., 1928; elk. to comanr., Aug., 1928.

WILLMOT, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1881; ed. Landing Coll. and Asptria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; astt. surrv., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. astt., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engnr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rlyw. constrn., Dec., 1913; served with 4th K.A.R. in E. African Campaign, May, 1915 to Dec., 1918 (twice ment. in desps.); ag. D.D.P.W., Oct., 1920 to Apr., 1922; D.D.P.W., June, 1923; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1923-24, 1926-27 and 1929.
WILMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P.—Astt. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913.

WILSON, BARL MAYOR, M.B., Ch.B. (1901), D.P.H. (1911), M.D. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Malvern Coll. (Faber Exhib.), and Univ. of Manchester; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1904; dist. med. offr., Levuka, 1907; dist. med. offr., Ba., 1915; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1918-19; served with B.E.F. in France; res. med. offr., col. hosp. Suva, M.O.H. and bacteriologist, Fiji, 1922; prim. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, mem., leg. coun., Br. Hond., 1923; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1925; nominated mem., leg. coun.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES.—B. 1879; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (six, jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., 11th Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; astt. contr. of lab., Penang, 18th Dec., 1914; passed cadet, 9th Jan., 1915; on milly. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., 22nd July, 1915; France, 13th Feb., 1916; offr., ols. V., 7th May, 1916; lieut., 7th July, 1916; capt., 13th Feb., 1919; demob., 16th Mar., 1919; ment. in desp., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, 14th June, 1919; offr., ols. IV., 11th Jan., 1920; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, in addn., during June, 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., 1st Sept. to 4th Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, 4th Dec., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., 6th Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., Malay Vol. Infy., Perak, 12th Apr., 1921; ag. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, 1st July, 1922; emigr. agt. in India, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. stamp and probate offr., Singapore, May, 1924; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; ag. dep. treas. (B), Singapore, in addn., Mar., 1925; ag. emigrn. comanr. for Malaya, Madras, Feb., 1926; accomp. contr., lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, May, 1929.
WILSON, C. J.—Medical offr., R.A.P., May, 1911; dep. dir., med. serv., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., med. and sany. servs., 1926; prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1928.

WILSON, CHARLES LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1874; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Grenada, as 2nd elk., G.P.O., Aug., 1892; ag. ch. elk., 1893; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; ex. off. sec., gen. bd. of health and bd. of educn., 1897-1903; ag. ch. elk. to gov. of Windward Is., 1907-8; ch. elk., govt. office, St. Vincent, elk. of couns., man. of printing sub-dept., and sec., hurricane loan bd., 1907-11; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, elk. of couns. and man., printing sub. dept., 1911-19; col. postmr., Grenada, 1919; a dir. of the pub. offrs. guarantee fund, 1919; sec. of reception comtes., visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to Grenada, Sept., 1920.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and elk., Br. Solomon Is., 25th June, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, 18th Jan., 1915 and 2nd Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, 15th Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., 7th May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., 20th Apr., 1919 and 6th Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st elk., treas. and cust., 11th Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., 26th Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, 2nd Apr., 1923; dep. comanr. for W. Pacifico, 27th July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, 10th Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., 6th Mar., 1925.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Amersham Gram. Schl.; elk. acctnt.gen.'s dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., O.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. astt. postmr.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang; astt. postmr.-gen., Singapore, 15th Feb., 1906; ag. astt. postmr.-gen., Penang, Apr., 1909; ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Jan., 1911; astt. postmr.-gen., Penang, 1st Jan., 1912, but still ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, Mar., 1912 to Mar., 1913; ag. postmr.-gen., S. Stilhms., Mar., 1914; promoted to Class III., May, 1915; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; elk., ch. sec.'s off., 1904; ag. astt. sec., ch. sec.'s off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. ch. elk., ch. sec.'s off., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. comanr. in 1923, 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; ch. collr., cust., and excise, July, 1924; regtr., Br. ships, regtr., Cyprus ships, imprsr., fisheries; ag. regtr., trademarks, ag. regtr., companies, ag. regtr., patents, July, 1924; mem. leg. coun., 1926; title altered to comptr., cust and excise, July, 1927.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; astt. dist. comanr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comanr., 1st Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy. capt., R.A.M.O., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripus, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry comm., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high comrn.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comtes.); retired; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.

WILSON, Sir Jeremiah, Kt. Bach. (1920), C.M.G. (1918).—Entd. Impl. telegraph ser., 22nd May, 1876; transf. to Cape telegraph ser., 1880; prin. clk., foreign mails branch, 1883; chief clk., G.P.O., 1898; ast. sec., 1906; sec., 1st Feb., 1908; under sec., Union admin., 31st May, 1910; postm.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910; ret., Dec., 1920.

WILSON, Hon. John Carnegie Dove, K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1865; ed. privately and at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs., advocate of the Scottish bar; second puisne judge of sup. ct. Natal, Oct., 1904; judge pres. of the sup. ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Division), January, 1911.

WILSON, Lieut.-Col. John Wolseley, M.C., V.D.—B. 1876; man., Tobago farm, 1899; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., 1905; inspr., 1907; senr. inspr., 1916; served with Imp. Army, 1914-19; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., 1920; ag. commdt., local forces on several occasions.

WILSON, Capt. Logie Weir.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Sch., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; ast. dist. comrn., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. comrn., Pujehun and Bonthe dists., 1921; dist. comrn., 1st Aug., 1926.

WILSON, Brig.-Gen. Sir Samuel Herbert, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major, 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1910; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1916; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comtes., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff offr., 1st grade, 1916; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comtes., 19th Nov., 1918; gov. of Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed gov't., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1926.

WILTSHIRE, Eric.—B. 1893; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; ast. engrn., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; ag. dist. engrn. in 1925, 1926 and 1927 ag. dist. engrn., Oct.-Dec., 1927 and in 1928; ag. ast. dir. in 1927 and 1928.

WINGATE, Andrew Frederick.—B. 1880; ed. Blundell's Sch.; on Indian rly., 1912-14; Guayaquil and Quito rly., 1914-19; traffic offr. Sierra Leone govt. rly., 1919-20; ast. traffic supt. Tanganyika Territory cent. rly., 1920.
WINSTER, Cyril Leigh, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O (1920).—Priv. sec. to govt. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, Victor Hantot, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffina Sch., Kingston on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrg.); 2nd cls. engrg., G.P.O., engrg. dept., Oct., 1909; ast. engrg., July, 1911; supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war surv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; ag. div., engrg., Penang, Nov., 1919-Jan., 1921; ag. supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Jan., 1921; supdt., ditto, Jan., 1922; ag. tels. engrg., posts and tels., F.M.S., Feb.-Sept., 1926; senr. engrg., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; ag. engrg.-in-ch., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct.-Dec., 1926; senr. engrg., posts and tels., Panang, July, 1928.

WINSTEDT, Richard Olaf, C.M.G. (1926) M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll. Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum. M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1903; ag. inspr. of schls., Perak; passed in Malay, 1904; int. law, Oct., 1904; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1904; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, Oct., 1909; ag. ast. dist. offr., Matang, and insp. of fisheries, Jan., 1908; clas. V., Jan., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng, Apr., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Sept., 1912; sec. to comtes. for Malay studies in addition, Sept., 1913; clas IV.; confirmed dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Dec., 1913; federal examiner in Malay in addition, Dec., 1913; promoted to clas III.; ast. dir. of education, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sanitary bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addition; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. dir., educn., S. Sttltns., and F.M.S., Feb., 1920; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Sttltns., and F.M.S., June, 1920; ag. chmn., comtes. for Malay studies, F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin. of Raffles Coll., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., May, 1923; offg. as sec. to high comrn. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addition, Mar., 1923; dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comtes. in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech., educn. comtes. in addn., Nov., 1925; rep. F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confere., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comtes., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi, a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; ast. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISE, Kenrick Stanton.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C., Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and registr.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, 4th Aug., 1919.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth (Gram. Sch.) and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. moda., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909 apptd. after compet. exam., olk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. olk., C.O., Moh., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comtes., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. olk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918). A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., rlyw. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 1911; dir., wireless telegraph, 1st Jan., 1914; 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WITTEENOEM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WODEHOUSE, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSCHINE HENEGH, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsnr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidiye; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WODEHOUSE, P. P. J., C.I.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first cls., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; ast. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; pol. mag., N.T. (Islands), Sept., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examns., 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907; 1908 to 1909, 1911 to 1912; census offr., 1911; dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1912; aide-de-camp to gov., 1918 to 1919; ag. capt. supt. of pol., on various occasions, 1918-25.

WODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office asst. to gov't. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. comsnr. of excise attached to the office of the excise comsnr., Apr., 1913; ast. comsnr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. gov't. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ag. ast. gov't. agt., Matale, Aug., 1915; attached to chief censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comsnr., excise, Nov., 1926; comsnr., excise, Sept., 1927.

WOLFE, EDWARD DUDLEY CORSOADEN, C.M.G. (1928).—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. lang., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. lang., tripes. (1896); 1st cls. Pt. I., and 2nd cls. Pt. II., Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry comm., 1901; regis., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. ast. regis.-gen., 1902; regis., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ast. supt. of pol., 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspnr. of schls., 1907; ag. regis.-gen., 1907; ast. regis.-gen., 1907; ag. inspnr. of schls., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; capt. supt. of pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. conf., New York, 1926.

WOLFE, HENRY, M.Sc. (A.J.C.) Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. ast., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17.

WOLFFE, PHINEAS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Academy; olk., land dept., R.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land ast., 1919; regis. of titles, documents and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, JAMES FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut., S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); ast. comsnr., pol., Kenya, Jan., 1927.

WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. ast. regis.-gen., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. ast. regis.-gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., N.T., 1910 to 1911; 2nd ast. regis.-gen., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, June, 1912; title of ast. regis.-gen. altered to 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1914; ag. dist. offr., 1915; ag. chief ast. to S.O.A., 1916, and 1918 to 1919; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipo, 1919 to 1920; ag. ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Taipo in addn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educn., Dec., 1925; dir., educn., Jan., 1926.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; ast. conservator, Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conservator, 1918; conservator, of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); ast. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1906; ch. ast., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. ast., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, JOHN CALDER, M.C., Qual. Land Surveyor, S. Africa—B., 1885; astt. R. Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; cadastral and trig. survr., F.M.S., 1911; war survr., France and England, 1915-19; offr.-in-ch., cadastral framework traverse, Gold Coast survey, 1923; dep. survr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripos, B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; regis. of land ct., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; astt. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. astt. reg.-gen., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. regar. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22 and in 1926; chmn., Sui fir puiycom. comm., 1922; puisne judge, 1926; ag. ch. just., 1927.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. tripos, pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. tripos., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; astt. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comnr., 1914-20; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of couns., Feb., 1922.

WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; astt. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.
WOODFORD, HON. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Harbor Main, Newfoundland, 1889; chrmn., bd. of works, 1897; min. of public works, 1898, 1909, and 1918; M.E.C., 1918.

WOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; astt. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, Nov., 1914; ment. in desps. for service in German E. Africa, Nov., 1916; astt. dist. comnr., Uganda, Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; dist. offr., June, 1926.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. pvt. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; astt. to R.A.M.C. at Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schl. of Trop. Med. exam., 1909; med offr. and radiologist and dist. comnr., Br. Honduras, 1910-16; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; ag. med. offr. of health and med. offr. of health, Kowloon, 1916; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.

WOODS, A. E., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1874; extra master's certif., 1897; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1903; lieut. 1906; lieut.-commdr., 1914; commr., 1919 (retd.); 1st and gunnery offr. H.M.S. "Naid" and "Oropesa," 1914-15; commanded H.M.S.Y. "St. George"'s.p. serv., Mediterranean, 1915; commanded destroyer divn., extended defence Channel patrol, 1917; ch. exam. offr., Gibraltar, 1918; mast. of "Lord Milner," Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; mast. of "Azania," Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

WOODS, SIR WILFRID WENTWORTH, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1928)—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schol., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Sept., 1901; astt. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; astt. col. auditor, Ceylon, Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at the War Office, Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. col. treas., May to Nov., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., June, 1921; ag. office astt. to govt.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addl. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1926; astt. govt. agt., N'Eliya, May, 1927; astt. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. comnr., Western Pacific, 1911; admstr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. comnr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. comnr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; res. comnr., Apr., 1919; judl. comnr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov. on various occasions, 1921-28.

WORLEY, NEWHAN ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; astt. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; astt. dist. offr., Ipo, May, 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Perak Dec., 1921; astt. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; ag. astt. dir. educn. (Chinese), S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1924; ag. prot. Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb., 1925 and Nov., 1926; astt. dir., educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1927; ag. dir., educn., F.M.S. and S.S., Apr., July, 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb. to July, 1928.

WORLLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in desps.; Ireland, 1919; astt. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; astt. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. astt. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. astt. audr., Nov., 1925.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 12th Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office astt. to govt. agt., W.
